## Goal
Take the existing `attendance-app/` Expo source (from [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5)) and produce a signed, sideloadable `attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk` wired to the demo Supabase URL deployment-engineer publishes on parent [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5).

## Coordination
- deployment-engineer (on [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5)) will post `demo_backend_url` as a metadata key + comment as soon as it's live. **Poll that issue every 5 min** until the URL appears, then proceed.
- Do NOT block on Firebase. Set `FCM_ENABLED=false` in `.env.production` — already supported by the FCM registration module (graceful no-op).
- Do NOT block on EAS / Expo cloud. Local build only.

## Build steps
1. `git clone` or restore the attendance-app worktree (check the project resource on App Hub `09e6cfd3-d45e-4225-a494-2e3400b6983c`). If unrecoverable, regenerate the scaffold from [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5) description — the AC list is the spec.
2. Install Android SDK 34 + build-tools 34.0.0 + platform-tools via `sdkmanager` (one-off). Java 17 is on the host already.
3. `expo prebuild --platform android --clean`
4. Edit `android/app/build.gradle` to ensure `versionCode 1`, `versionName "0.1.0-demo"`, applicationId `com.newtechkw.attendance.demo` (suffix `.demo` to allow side-by-side with future prod).
5. Generate debug keystore (or use the one shipped by `expo prebuild`): `keytool -genkey -v -keystore android/app/debug.keystore -alias androiddebugkey -storepass android -keypass android -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000 -dname "CN=Demo,OU=NewTech,O=NewTech,L=Kuwait,ST=KW,C=KW"`
6. `cd android && ./gradlew assembleRelease` (release variant signed with debug keystore — fine for sideload).
7. Output: `android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk` → rename to `attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk`.
8. Attach the APK to **[DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5)** via `multica issue comment add ... --attachment ./attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk`.
9. Smoke test: install on Android emulator (API 34, x86_64) via `adb install -r`, launch, confirm login screen renders in RTL Arabic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] APK file ≤ 60 MB
- [ ] `adb install` succeeds on a fresh emulator
- [ ] App launches without crash; login screen visible in Arabic RTL
- [ ] Attempting login with seeded `super_admin@diwan.demo / Demo2026!` returns a session token from the demo backend

## Self-heal
- Two `gradle assembleRelease` failures with the same error → swap to `npx eas-cli build --platform android --profile preview --local` (still local, no cloud) before escalating.
- If Android SDK install fails on this runtime, escalate to [@ceo](mention://agent/d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) with the exact error — we'll swap to a runtime that has it pre-installed.

## DoD
APK attached to [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5) + smoke-test screenshot + status flipped to `in_review`. CEO picks it up from there and pushes to user.

issues.description:
## Goal (single sentence)
Ship the user a downloadable, installable **`.apk` that runs on a clean Android device today**, backed by a self-hosted demo Supabase + stubbed FCM, so [admin@newtechkw.com](mention://member/0c06df4b-79c1-44d4-a0a0-a822d82ae6f4) can perform full UI / offline / RTL / golden-path testing without waiting for paid resources.

## Why now
User asked on [DEV-36](mention://issue/e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) (parent comment `8f6ce1bf`) for the complete app ready for full Android testing **now**. DEV-33 is code-done, QA-gate-passed (13/13 tests), but no APK exists yet. External-credential ask is in flight in parallel; this issue is the **action path that does NOT wait on the user**.

## Inputs (already available)
- React Native Expo source from [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5) (in mobile-engineer's last worktree — re-checkout from the App Hub project `09e6cfd3-d45e-4225-a494-2e3400b6983c` if cleared)
- Schema + migrations from [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76)
- Edge Functions from [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37)
- HR Admin Web from [DEV-34](mention://issue/3f3a89bb-3322-4f87-bd97-f6e89cf63d9b) (separate preview URL acceptable)
- Deploy plan from [DEV-39](mention://issue/b2fc5cb4-f977-421f-95be-6d9e18738b22) `DEPLOY.md` + `RUNBOOK.md`

## Expected Output
1. **`attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk`** — debug-signed, sideloadable, installable on Android API 24+.
   - Embedded `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` pointing at a **self-hosted Supabase instance** (Docker compose on this runtime host, exposed via Cloudflare tunnel — no paid account needed).
   - FCM stubbed (`FCM_ENABLED=false`) → notifications surface as in-app toasts; no Firebase project required.
   - Embedded demo seed: 1 tenant (شركة الديوان للتجارة), 3 offices (Kuwait City / Salmiya / Hawalli), 5 shifts, 10 employees, 1 super_admin + 2 hr_manager + 7 employees with pre-set passwords.
2. **`HOW_TO_INSTALL.ar.md`** — Kuwaiti Arabic walkthrough: enable "تطبيقات من مصادر غير معروفة", install APK, login as either `super_admin@diwan.demo / Demo2026!` or `employee01@diwan.demo / Demo2026!`, click through full golden path.
3. **Admin web preview URL** — Vercel preview (`vercel deploy` without `--prod`, free tier OK with existing account or a fresh email; if blocked, fall back to ngrok of local `pnpm dev`).
4. **Self-hosted Supabase backend URL** posted as `demo_backend_url` metadata on this issue. Must stay live ≥ 7 days for user testing.
5. **APK attached** to this issue as a file, plus a public download mirror (Cloudflare R2 / temp file.io / GitHub release on `adminnewtech` if PAT exists) for easy phone download.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] APK installs on a clean Android emulator (API 24+) without errors
- [ ] Login → Check-In (GPS happy path with seeded office geofence) → Check-Out → History → Leave Request all work end-to-end
- [ ] Offline test passes: airplane mode → punch → reconnect → syncs without duplicate
- [ ] RTL Arabic Kuwaiti layout is the default on first launch
- [ ] Admin web preview URL is reachable and HR manager seed account can log in, see today's punches, approve a leave
- [ ] `HOW_TO_INSTALL.ar.md` is genuinely Kuwaiti tone (not MSA), tested by [@arabic-rtl-experience-agent](mention://agent/7bcef3ff-a4d6-4ea3-866e-bdcc00b27576)
- [ ] No secrets in the APK, source, or any committed artifact — only demo credentials labelled as demo

## Build approach (recommended)
- **No EAS / no Expo cloud build.** Use `expo prebuild` → local `./gradlew assembleRelease` with debug keystore (`-keystore debug.keystore -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000 -keypass android -storepass android -dname "CN=Demo"`). Install Android SDK 34 + build-tools 34.0.0 + platform-tools via `sdkmanager` if not present on the runtime host (one-time cost).
- **Self-hosted Supabase via `supabase/postgres` + `supabase/gotrue` + `supabase/postgrest` + `supabase/storage-api` containers**, all from the official `docker-compose` recipe. Exposed via Cloudflare tunnel (free, no card required).
- **Apply migrations from [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76) and Edge Functions from [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37) on the self-hosted instance**, then seed with `seed.sql` (deliverable in this task).

## Dependencies
None blocking on user. All work happens on this runtime host or free-tier infra.

## Risk
- Self-hosted Supabase will not survive a runtime restart unless persisted to /opt/data — mount volumes and document the restart procedure in `RUNBOOK.md`.
- Cloudflare tunnel free tier requires a `cloudflared` daemon — if not installable, fall back to `ngrok` (also free with a temp account).
- APK signed with debug keystore CANNOT be uploaded to Play Store — that's fine, this is for sideloading only. Mark APK filename and the install guide with **"DEMO BUILD — NOT FOR PLAY STORE"**.

## Definition of Done
APK link posted as a top-level comment on **[DEV-36](mention://issue/e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b)** (the user's parent thread) with: install instructions in Kuwaiti Arabic, demo accounts list, backend URL, preview URL for admin web, a 90-second test checklist, and a known-limitations note. `pipeline_status=demo-apk-ready` pinned on DEV-25 metadata once delivered.

## Coordination
- **deployment-engineer** (assignee) owns: self-hosted Supabase + Cloudflare tunnel + seed data + admin web preview URL + APK hosting mirror.
- **mobile-engineer** (sub-task or peer dispatch): the actual APK build — `expo prebuild` → gradle assembleRelease — wired to the backend URL deployment-engineer provisions.
- Coordinate via this thread; deployment-engineer pings mobile-engineer with the backend URL as soon as it's live so the APK build can embed it.

Self-heal: if `gradle assembleRelease` fails twice with the same error on this runtime, swap to `expo-cli` cloud build (anonymous EAS) before escalating. Document the swap in `retry_log` metadata.

## Input
Parent: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794). All build sub-issues (DEV-31..35).

This is the **full-app QA test plan** — covers the integration of all builds, not the per-build unit tests (those landed in DEV-31..35 individually).

## Expected Output

A `qa-test-plan.md` in the repo with:

1. **Golden path E2E**: employee logs in → checks in (with geofence + selfie) → checks out → sees correct hours → submits leave request → HR approves → employee receives push → employee sees decision in app.
2. **Anti-golden path**:
   - Outside geofence → check-in rejected with Arabic error
   - Rate-limit on punch endpoint (1 per 30s) blocks fast double-punch
   - Offline mode queues punch → reconnects → syncs without duplicate
   - JWT expired mid-session → app refreshes silently (no re-login UX)
   - Civil ID masked correctly per role
3. **Permission matrix tests**: each of the 4 roles attempts every action; row × column = pass/fail
4. **RLS smoke**: no cross-tenant data leak (tenant A signing in cannot see tenant B's employees)
5. **WCAG 2.2 AA**: keyboard nav on admin dashboard, ARIA labels, color contrast, RTL active
6. **Performance**:
   - p95 punch latency ≤ 800ms
   - Live board update ≤ 3s after punch event
   - Monthly report (500 employees × 31 days) generates < 30s
7. **Security checks** (top up the per-build gates):
   - SQL injection on every parameterised endpoint
   - XSS on every text input rendered in the admin dashboard
   - CSRF / replay attack on punch endpoint with stolen JWT
   - FCM token rotation honoured (old token deleted on rotation)
8. **Device matrix**: Android 7 / 10 / 13 / 14 + emulator + at least one physical device

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each test has clear Given/When/Then + expected pass criteria
- [ ] Each test mapped to the specific Acceptance Criterion in its source issue (DEV-31..35)
- [ ] Section "Manual smoke tests" lists the 5 minute post-deploy check
- [ ] Plan is executable by a non-author (clear, no assumed context)

## Dependencies
DEV-33 (mobile build) and DEV-34 (admin dashboard) must be ready to install/access before EXECUTION. Plan AUTHORING can start now.

## Risk
- Physical Android device for testing — flag if not available (emulator coverage is acceptable for MVP per PRD).
- Firebase provisioning still pending → push tests in section 1 cannot execute until DEV-35 Firebase Vault is loaded.

## Definition of Done
`qa-test-plan.md` committed. Comment on this issue with file link + summary of coverage map. Plan reviewed and signed off by [@quality-gate-manager](mention://agent/d87c831d-bd4d-459f-9672-d633e238c44f).

# Skill — `app-hub-bootstrap`

**Trigger:** A new App Request lands in `01 - App Requests` AND classifier outputs `vertical: <any>`. Run **before** `app-idea-to-product-brief`.

**Required input:** App slug (kebab-case), display name (English + Arabic), one-line purpose.

## Steps

1. **Create the App Hub project**
   ```bash
   multica project create \
     --title "APP — <Display Name>" \
     --icon "<emoji>" \
     --description "App Hub for <Display Name>. All execution lives here (per ADR-002)." \
     --lead ceo \
     --output json
   ```
   Capture the returned `id` as `<APP_PROJECT_ID>`.

2. **Provision the GitHub repo** (escalates to admin if no PAT in runtime env)
   - `gh repo create newtechkw/<slug> --template newtechkw/app-template --private --description "<one-liner>"`
   - Seed branches: `main` (default, protected) + `integrate` (continuous-integration target).
   - If `GH_TOKEN` is missing → open an escalation sub-issue on the parent App Request mentioning workspace-operations-manager with the exact `gh` command, **do not proceed without the repo**.

3. **Attach repo to hub**
   ```bash
   multica project resource add <APP_PROJECT_ID> \
     --type github_repo \
     --url https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug> \
     --default-branch-hint main
   ```

4. **Initialize the codegraph baseline**
   ```bash
   multica repo checkout https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug> --ref main
   cd <slug>
   codegraph init && codegraph index
   npx understand-anything init && npx understand-anything scan
   git add .codegraph .understand && git commit -m "chore: codegraph + understand baseline" && git push
   ```

5. **Pin the metadata contract on the parent App Request issue**
   - `app_project_id=<APP_PROJECT_ID>`
   - `app_repo_url=https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug>`
   - `lifecycle_phase=intake`
   - `integration_branch=integrate`
   - `local_run_command=<inferred from template>` (e.g. `pnpm dev`, `expo start`)
   - `deploy_gate=blocked: pre-build`
   - `codegraph_status=fresh@<ISO>` (set by the init in step 4)

6. **Hand off to Product UX Squad** by reassigning the parent issue.
   The Product UX Squad runs `app-idea-to-product-brief` and writes the brief **inside the App Hub project**, not in `02 - Product Specs`.

## Output

- One Multica project (`APP — <name>`) ready to accept sub-issues.
- One GitHub repo with codegraph + understand baseline.
- Parent App Request issue carries the full metadata contract.
- Comment on the parent: "App Hub bootstrap complete. Project: [link]. Repo: <url>. Handing to Product UX."

## Quality checklist

- [ ] Project exists with correct title + icon + lead.
- [ ] Repo exists, both branches present, branch protection on `main`.
- [ ] `.codegraph/codegraph.db` and `.understand/graph.json` exist on `main`.
- [ ] Parent issue has all 7 metadata keys pinned.
- [ ] Repo attached to project (visible in `multica project resource list <APP_PROJECT_ID>`).

## Failure handling

- Missing GH PAT → escalation issue + halt. Do not silently skip the repo step.
- Repo create failed (name taken) → append `-v2` and retry; pin the actual name in metadata.
- codegraph install missing on runtime → open issue against workspace-operations-manager to install on the runtime image.

## Improvement rule

If the same app vertical (e.g. CRM, HR, delivery) bootstraps ≥3 times with the same template, promote that template variant via `template-builder` (e.g. `app-template-crm`, `app-template-hr`).

issues.description:
# ADR-002 — App Hub Model (per-app project + per-app repo + codegraph brain + local-first lifecycle)

**Status:** Adopted 2026-05-25.
**Supersedes:** Default routing where every Build child lands in `05 - Parallel Build`.
**Driver:** [DEV-36](mention://issue/e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) — workspace owner asked for a coherent per-project structure, local-first integration, single integrated deploy at the end, and a code-graph layer in the style of [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) and [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph).

---

## 1. The change in one line
**Every app/system gets its own Multica project + its own GitHub repo + its own codegraph index. Phase projects (00–09) become the workspace's *library*, not the place where work lives.**

---

## 2. New project taxonomy

| Layer | Purpose | Examples |
|-------|---------|----------|
| **Library projects** (`00 - Factory Brain` … `09 - Research Intelligence`) | Standards, ADRs, skills, templates, research. Long-lived. Cross-app. | ADRs, the Skill Library, intake forms. |
| **App Hub projects** (`APP — <name>`) | One per app/system. **All execution issues for that app live here** — PRD, schema, build, QA, deploy, docs. Closes when the app is shipped. | `APP — Android Attendance`, `APP — HR Dashboard`, … |

The current scattered routing (PRD in `02`, schema in `04`, build children in `05`, QA in `06`, deploy in `07`) is replaced with: **every artifact for app X lives in `APP — X` and carries a `phase=<intake|spec|design|arch|build|integrate|qa|deploy|done>` label.** Cross-app skill/standard updates still live in the library projects.

## 3. Per-app GitHub repo

- Convention: `newtechkw/<app-slug>` (e.g. `newtechkw/attendance-app`).
- Created **once** at the Architecture Gate by `deployment-engineer` (escalation if no PAT).
- Attached to the app hub via `multica project resource add <hub-project-id> --type github_repo --url <repo-url>`.
- Default branch `main`. Two protected branches: `main` (deploy target) and `integrate` (continuous integration target).
- Pinned on the app's parent issue as `app_repo_url`.

## 4. Local-first lifecycle (the hard rule)

Build engineers run **everything locally** until the parent is in `in_review` with QA + Security green. No `vercel deploy`, no `eas build --profile production`, no `npm publish`, no `supabase db push --linked`. Preview deploys (`vercel --prod=false`, `eas build --profile preview`, local Supabase via Docker) are allowed and encouraged for integration testing.

Phases (mirrored in the parent issue metadata `lifecycle_phase`):

```
intake → spec → design → arch → build → integrate → qa → deploy → done
```

- `build` ends only when every build sub-issue is `done` AND the codegraph reports no orphan endpoints / missing handlers.
- `integrate` = one combined PR (or one weekly integration PR) merging every build stream into `integrate` branch + green local E2E run.
- `qa` = QA Security Squad veto gate on the `integrate` branch, not on a deployed environment.
- `deploy` = **single integrated deploy**, owned by DevOps Launch Squad, executes only after QA + Security sign-off on `integrate`.

## 5. Codegraph layer (the shared brain)

Inspired by [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph) (SQLite + tree-sitter + MCP) and [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) (multi-agent indexer + JSON knowledge graph).

Per repo:
- `.codegraph/codegraph.db` (SQLite, tree-sitter symbol/edge graph, FTS5).
- `.understand/graph.json` (architectural overview + business-domain map).
- Refreshed by the `codegraph-indexer` agent on every PR merge to `integrate` (and on demand).
- Exposed to other agents via MCP (`codegraph_search`, `codegraph_trace`, `codegraph_callers`, `codegraph_impact`) — wires into Build + QA agents so they reason on indexed symbols instead of grepping. Net effect from the upstream project: ~35% fewer tokens, ~71% fewer tool calls.

Owner: **Knowledge Graph Squad** (new — see §7).

## 6. Per-app metadata contract

Pinned on the **app's parent issue** (e.g. DEV-25):

| Key | Example | Why |
|-----|---------|-----|
| `app_project_id` | `<hub-uuid>` | Pointer to the per-app Multica project. |
| `app_repo_url` | `https://github.com/newtechkw/attendance-app` | Single source of truth for the codebase. |
| `lifecycle_phase` | `build` | Drives Factory Pulse routing. |
| `local_run_command` | `pnpm dev` or `expo start` | What a fresh agent runs to verify locally. |
| `integration_branch` | `integrate` | QA target before deploy. |
| `codegraph_status` | `fresh@2026-05-25T18:00Z` | Last indexer run timestamp. |
| `deploy_gate` | `blocked: qa-pending` / `green` | Hard gate read by `deployment-engineer`. |
| `waiting_on` | (existing) | External blockers (credentials, paid resources). |

## 7. New squad: Knowledge Graph Squad

Members:
- **codegraph-indexer** (leader) — runs codegraph + understand-anything pipelines on PR merge, refreshes `.codegraph/codegraph.db` and `.understand/graph.json`, posts a diff-impact summary on the merge issue.
- **codebase-cartographer** — owns the human-readable architecture overview (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`), the guided tours, the symbol naming conventions, and the cross-repo symbol vocabulary.
- **impact-analyst** — on every Build sub-issue, runs `codegraph_impact` against the proposed diff scope and posts the affected-symbols list so reviewers know the blast radius.

Trigger: PR opened or merged on any `APP — *` repo. Skill: `codegraph-index-refresh` (to be authored).

## 8. New skill: `app-hub-bootstrap`

Steps:
1. Create `APP — <name>` Multica project (icon, description, lead = ceo).
2. Provision GitHub repo `newtechkw/<slug>` from template (template repo to be set up: includes Next.js + Supabase + Expo skeleton + codegraph install script + Understand-Anything CLI). **Requires GH PAT.**
3. Attach repo to project via `multica project resource add`.
4. Seed `main` and `integrate` branches.
5. Run `codegraph init` + `understand-anything init` on the empty repo so future merges have a baseline.
6. Pin the metadata contract on the parent app issue.
7. Hand to Product UX Squad for PRD.

## 9. Factory Pulse v3 (incremental, not destructive)

Add to existing autopilot **without changing current exclusions**:
- Read every issue's `app_project_id` metadata; route dispatch by `lifecycle_phase` first, falling back to current rules.
- New rule: when parent `lifecycle_phase=build` and every build child is `done`, flip to `integrate`, comment "Build complete — opening integration PR" and reassign the parent to Knowledge Graph Squad to run impact analysis + open the integration PR.
- New rule: never reassign the parent to DevOps Launch Squad until both `deploy_gate=green` AND `lifecycle_phase=qa→deploy`.
- DEV-36 + member-conversation exclusions remain.

## 10. What requires admin (credentials)

These are the only blockers — every other piece of this ADR is executable today:

1. **GitHub PAT** with `repo:create` scope on `newtechkw` org (so `app-hub-bootstrap` can create repos unattended).
2. **Codegraph + Understand-Anything install on the runtime host** — both are MIT-licensed npm/Python packages; the install is reversible but it modifies the runtime image.
3. Re-confirmation that **`newtechkw` is the canonical GitHub org**, not a personal account.

## 11. Migration plan for the apps already in flight

- **DEV-25 (Android Attendance)** — create `APP — Android Attendance` hub now, link via metadata, leave the in-flight children (DEV-26…DEV-35) where they are to avoid disrupting Factory Pulse v2 mid-build. Future children attach to the hub.
- Next inbound app uses the full hub model from intake.

## 12. Rejected alternatives

- **One mega-repo for all apps.** Rejected: codegraph index becomes 10× larger, cross-app blast radius hides real impact, deploy of one app forces a re-deploy of everything.
- **Keep using phase projects for execution + attach repo at workspace level.** Rejected: today's pain (no per-app container, scattered children, no per-app codegraph) doesn't go away.
- **Build the codegraph layer in-house.** Rejected: both upstream projects are MIT, mature enough, and battle-tested with agentic users — adopting beats rebuilding.

---

## Definition of Done for this ADR
- `APP — Android Attendance` project exists and DEV-25 has the metadata contract pinned. ✅ (executed in this run.)
- Knowledge Graph Squad + codegraph-indexer agent exist. ✅
- `app-hub-bootstrap` skill issue exists in `08 - Skill Library`. ✅
- Factory Pulse v3 patch drafted (deployed in a follow-up after user nod). ⏳
- GitHub repo provisioning escalation logged on DEV-25. ⏳ (requires admin.)

## Input
Architecture: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) comment #5 (push notification architecture). Schema: [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76). API: [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37).

**Blocked on**: Firebase project provisioning (requires admin approval — paid-free-tier resource).

## Expected Output
1. Firebase project created with Android app (package: `com.newtechkw.attendance`)
2. FCM Server Key stored in Supabase Vault as `fcm_server_key`
3. Edge Functions (already stubbed in DEV-32):
   - `notify-shift-reminder`: sends FCM 30 min before shift start; time-window logic; skips if already checked in
   - `notify-missed-punch-alert`: sends FCM 15 min after shift start if no check_in; skips if on approved leave
   - `notify-leave-decision`: sends approved/rejected push with reviewer note
4. pg_cron jobs registered:
   - `shift-reminder` every minute calling `notify-shift-reminder`
   - `missed-punch-alert` every minute calling `notify-missed-punch-alert`
5. Leave decision trigger: `leave_decision_notify` on `leave_requests` calling `notify-leave-decision`
6. All notifications use FCM HTTP v1 API (not legacy), `android.priority: high`
7. Notification payloads bilingual (Arabic body + English in `data` field for analytics)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shift reminder FCM received on device 30 ± 2 minutes before shift start
- [ ] Missed punch alert NOT sent if employee already checked in
- [ ] Leave decision push delivered within 10 seconds of HR approval
- [ ] FCM token rotation handled (old token deleted, new token registered on callback)
- [ ] pg_cron jobs verified running via `SELECT * FROM cron.job`
- [ ] FCM Server Key confirmed in Vault — not in source code or git history

## Dependencies
1. **Firebase project created by admin** (external blocker — promote to `todo` only after this lands)
2. DEV-31 schema (push_subscriptions table)
3. DEV-32 API (notify Edge Functions stubbed)
4. pg_net extension enabled on Supabase project

## Risk
- pg_cron fires every minute for ALL tenants — at scale, batch FCM sends via `messages` batch API to avoid rate limits.
- FCM token may be stale — implement silent push to verify token validity monthly.

## Definition of Done
End-to-end test: employee logged into app receives shift reminder 30 min before start, missed punch alert if no check-in, and leave decision push within 10s of HR approval. All three notification paths verified on physical Android device.

## Input
PRD: [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f). Architecture: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219). Schema: [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76). API: [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37).

## Expected Output
Next.js 14+ (App Router) admin web dashboard deployed on Vercel:

**Pages / Features**:
1. Login — Supabase Auth email + password; session via @supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs
2. Live Attendance Board — real-time grid via Supabase Realtime WebSocket; status badges (حاضر / متأخر / غائب / إجازة); department filter; auto-refresh ≤3s latency
3. Employee Management — data table with search/filter; create (with civil ID input, encrypted before save), edit, deactivate; photo upload to Supabase Storage
4. Office Management — CRUD with interactive map (Google Maps or Leaflet); geofence radius slider; GPS coordinate input
5. Shift Templates — CRUD form: name, start, end, grace minutes, break minutes; shift assignment to employees
6. Reports — date range picker; employee / department filter; CSV download (direct) + PDF download (async, signed URL); civil ID masked for non-HR-Manager roles
7. Leave Approval — pending requests queue; approve / reject with optional note; filtered by dept for Dept Manager
8. Audit Log — paginated table (100/page); filters: actor, date range, action type; CSV export

**Technical requirements**:
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript
- @supabase/ssr for server-side Supabase client (RLS-enforced queries)
- Supabase Realtime client for live board WebSocket subscription
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui component library
- React Query (TanStack) for client-side data fetching
- react-hook-form + zod for form validation
- i18n: next-intl, Arabic Kuwaiti default, English toggle, RTL via dir='rtl' on <html>
- Role-based route guards: middleware.ts checks JWT role claim; redirects unauthorized access
- Civil ID: never stored or displayed in plaintext on frontend — always masked (****XXXX) except in HR Manager employee detail page where full value fetched via Edge Function
- WCAG 2.2 AA: keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, sufficient contrast

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Live board updates within 3 seconds of a punch event via Supabase Realtime
- [ ] Dept Manager sees only own department employees on live board and leave queue
- [ ] Civil ID displayed as ****XXXX for all roles except hr_manager/super_admin viewing employee detail
- [ ] Monthly report (500 employees, 31 days) generates and starts download within 30 seconds
- [ ] Route guard blocks Employee role from accessing /admin/* routes
- [ ] RTL layout active when Arabic locale is selected
- [ ] Deployed to Vercel with preview URL

## Dependencies
Supabase project URL + anon key (public) + service_role key (Vercel env only). DEV-31 (schema). DEV-32 (API Edge Functions for reports, invite). Google Maps API key for office geofence map.

## Risk
- Google Maps API key requires billing account — use Leaflet + OpenStreetMap as fallback for MVP.
- PDF report generation timeout on large datasets — trigger async job (202 response) and poll for signed URL.

## Definition of Done
All 8 feature areas implemented. Deployed to Vercel preview URL. Happy path tested: create employee → assign shift → employee punches in → live board shows update → HR runs monthly report → downloads CSV.

## Input
PRD: [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f). Architecture: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219). Schema: [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76). API: [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37).

## Expected Output
React Native Expo application (Android target, API 24+) with:

**Screens**:
1. Login screen — email + password; magic link fallback; Arabic-first RTL layout
2. Home / Today's card — status (لم يبدأ / حاضر / انصرف), check-in time, hours worked; Check In / Check Out buttons
3. Check-In flow — GPS validation (Haversine client-side pre-check) → optional selfie capture → optional QR scan → POST punch-checkin → result feedback
4. Attendance history — 30-day list; status badge; tap for detail (GPS mini-map + selfie thumbnail)
5. Shift schedule — this week + next week; shift name, start, end, office
6. Leave requests — list + submit form (type, date range, note); status badge
7. Offline sync indicator — badge count + sync status bar

**Technical requirements**:
- Expo SDK 51+ (bare or managed workflow; bare preferred for GPS + camera)
- Supabase JS client with JWT session management (auto-refresh)
- expo-location for GPS (foreground only — no background tracking)
- expo-camera for selfie capture; compress to ≤200 KB before upload
- expo-barcode-scanner for QR check-in
- expo-sqlite for offline punch queue persistence
- expo-notifications + @react-native-firebase/messaging for FCM token registration
- @react-navigation/native (bottom tabs + stack)
- i18n: react-i18next, Arabic Kuwaiti strings default, English toggle, RTL-first via I18nManager.forceRTL
- Zustand or Redux Toolkit for auth/session state
- React Query (TanStack) for server state
- WCAG 2.2 AA: minimum touch targets 44×44pt, sufficient color contrast

**Offline behaviour**:
- expo-sqlite stores queued punches (schema from DEV-30 offline sync spec)
- NetInfo listener triggers sync on reconnect
- Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff (30s → 120s → 300s)
- Failed punches show HR-contact prompt

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Check-In shows Arabic error 'أنت خارج نطاق المكتب' when outside geofence
- [ ] Punch queued to SQLite when offline; synced on reconnect with badge updating to 0
- [ ] Selfie compressed to ≤200 KB before upload
- [ ] App switches to full RTL layout when Arabic is active
- [ ] Session auto-refreshes without requiring re-login within 30-day window
- [ ] No background location access (foreground-only)
- [ ] EAS build produces a runnable APK on Android API 24+

## Dependencies
Supabase project URL + anon key. DEV-31 (schema) for real data; can build against mock/stub data initially. DEV-32 (API) for punch endpoints.

## Risk
- GPS accuracy indoors — QR scan is the fallback (opt-in per tenant); document this in release notes.
- Selfie camera permission UX — must explain purpose clearly per PDPL consent flow.

## Definition of Done
EAS internal build produces installable APK. Full happy path (login → check-in → check-out → history → leave request) tested on physical Android device or emulator. RTL layout verified. Offline queue sync verified with airplane mode test.

## Input
Architecture spec: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) comment #4 (API contract). Schema must exist (DEV-31).

## Expected Output
Supabase Edge Functions (Deno/TypeScript) for all custom endpoints:

1. `punch-checkin` — GPS geofence validation, rate limiting (punch_rate_limit table), selfie upload, lateness computation, idempotency via client_punch_id.
2. `punch-checkout` — active check-in verification, rate limiting, hours worked calculation.
3. `punch-sync` — batch offline queue sync, chronological ordering, idempotency, per-punch result map.
4. `employees/:id/invite` — Supabase Auth invite by email, invited_at update.
5. `reports/attendance` — CSV stream + async PDF generation (react-pdf), role-scoped data, civil ID masking.
6. `fcm-register` — FCM token upsert in push_subscriptions.
7. `notify-shift-reminder` — query employees with shifts starting in 29–31 min window, send FCM.
8. `notify-missed-punch-alert` — query employees whose shift started 14–16 min ago with no check_in, send FCM.
9. `notify-leave-decision` — receive leave_request_id, fetch status, send FCM to employee.

All Edge Functions must:
- Validate JWT claims via Supabase Auth verification
- Return bilingual error responses (Arabic + English) per DEV-30 error format
- Write to `audit_log` for all mutations
- Handle civil ID via `pgp_sym_decrypt` with key from Vault (never hardcoded)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `punch-checkin` returns 422 when employee is outside geofence
- [ ] `punch-checkin` returns 429 when called within 30s of last punch
- [ ] `punch-sync` processes punches in chronological order and skips duplicates
- [ ] `reports/attendance` masks civil ID as `****XXXX` for roles below hr_manager
- [ ] All functions reject unauthenticated requests with 401
- [ ] `notify-shift-reminder` sends FCM via Firebase HTTP v1 API (not legacy)
- [ ] Haversine distance calculation correct to within 1 meter

## Dependencies
[DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76) schema must land before integration testing. FCM Server Key in Supabase Vault (from Integration Architect / admin).

## Risk
- PDF generation in Deno Edge Function may hit memory limits on Supabase free tier — fallback: generate CSV only for MVP and defer PDF to a background job.
- pg_cron calls to Edge Functions require `pg_net` extension — confirm enabled on Supabase project.

## Definition of Done
All Edge Functions deployed to Supabase. Each endpoint tested with a real JWT from the test seed data (DEV-31). Postman/Bruno collection added to repo.

## Input
Architecture spec: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) comments #2 (schema DDL), #3 (RLS + auth hook).

Full schema DDL, RLS policies, helper functions, audit trigger, and JWT custom claims hook are documented in DEV-30.

## Expected Output
1. Supabase migration file(s) with all DDL: `tenants`, `departments`, `employees`, `offices`, `shifts`, `employee_shifts`, `punch_events`, `leave_requests`, `audit_log`, `push_subscriptions`, `punch_rate_limit`.
2. All RLS policies per DEV-30 comment #3.
3. Helper functions: `auth_tenant_id()`, `auth_employee_id()`, `auth_role()`, `auth_department_id()`, `is_admin_or_hr()`.
4. Auth Hook function: `auth.custom_access_token_hook` registered in Supabase dashboard.
5. Audit trigger `write_audit_log()` applied to: `employees`, `leave_requests`, `offices`, `shifts`.
6. Extensions enabled: `pgcrypto`, `pg_cron`, `btree_gist`.
7. Seed data: one test tenant + one employee per role (super_admin, hr_manager, dept_manager, employee) for QA use.
8. Migration tested: run on a clean Supabase project with no errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every table has `tenant_id` column and RLS enabled
- [ ] `employees.civil_id_encrypted` uses `pgp_sym_encrypt` with key from Vault — key is never in source code
- [ ] `employee_shifts` GIST exclusion prevents overlapping shift assignments
- [ ] `punch_events.client_punch_id` has UNIQUE constraint
- [ ] Auth hook injects `tenant_id`, `employee_id`, `role`, `department_id` into JWT `app_metadata`
- [ ] RLS policies match the matrix in DEV-30 comment #3 — no cross-tenant reads possible
- [ ] Audit trigger fires on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for all listed tables
- [ ] Migration file is idempotent (can be re-run safely)

## Dependencies
Architecture spec in [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219). Supabase project URL + service role key from admin (request if not yet provided).

## Risk
- Supabase Vault setup requires admin access to the Supabase dashboard — if not yet available, store key in `app.settings` temporarily and flag for migration.
- `btree_gist` extension may not be available on Supabase free tier — fallback: application-level overlap check in the employee_shifts API.

## Definition of Done
Migration files committed to repo. Migration applied to Supabase dev project with zero errors. Auth hook registered and verified via a test token decode showing correct `app_metadata` claims. Seed data present.

issues.description:
## Input
Parent: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794) | PRD: [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f)

Full PRD, user stories, permission matrix, and MVP scope cut are in DEV-26 comments.

## Expected Output
1. **Stack confirmation** — React Native Expo (Android) + Next.js + Supabase (Postgres + Auth + RLS + Realtime + Storage) + Firebase FCM + Vercel + EAS.
2. **Database schema** — all tables with columns, types, constraints, foreign keys, indexes. Tables: tenants, employees, offices, shifts, employee_shifts, punch_events, leave_requests, audit_log. Every table has tenant_id + RLS policy.
3. **RLS policies** — per table, per role (Super Admin / HR Manager / Dept Manager / Employee). Dept Manager filtered by department_id. Cross-tenant queries must be impossible.
4. **API contract** (OpenAPI 3.1 or equivalent) — all endpoints: auth, punch (check-in/out), punch queue sync, employees CRUD, offices CRUD, shifts CRUD, leave requests, leave approval, reports, audit log.
5. **Auth model** — Supabase Auth JWT claims, custom claims for role + tenant_id + department_id, session management.
6. **Offline sync design** — punch queue schema (local), conflict resolution strategy, retry policy (3 attempts, exponential backoff).
7. **Push notification architecture** — FCM integration, notification triggers (shift reminder, missed punch, leave decision), scheduled jobs.
8. **Security model** — encryption at rest for civil ID (AES-256), selfie storage policy (private bucket, signed URLs, 90-day TTL), TLS 1.3 in transit, rate limit on punch endpoint (1/30s per employee).
9. **Infrastructure diagram** — Supabase + Vercel + EAS + Firebase topology.
10. **PDPL compliance notes** — data classification, consent flows, breach notification path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every table in schema has tenant_id and RLS policy
- [ ] OpenAPI contract covers all MVP endpoints
- [ ] Civil ID encryption documented at column level
- [ ] Offline sync conflict resolution strategy is explicit
- [ ] Auth JWT claims include role, tenant_id, department_id

## Compliance Requirements
- PDPL Kuwait: biometric (selfie), location (GPS punch), civil ID are sensitive data — explicit consent flows required.
- RLS: cross-tenant data leakage is a P0 security defect.
- Civil ID: encrypted at rest, masked in API responses for roles below HR Manager.

## Dependencies
PRD approved in DEV-26.

## Definition of Done
Schema + RLS policies + API contract + auth model + offline sync design + security model posted as comments on this issue.

issues.description:
## Input
Parent App Request: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794).
PRD sub-issue (must land first): [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f).

Two surfaces:
- **Employee Android app** (React Native + Expo) — primary, mobile-only, RTL-first.
- **HR Admin web dashboard** (Next.js) — desktop primary, tablet supported.

## Expected Output
UX Architect (lead) coordinates UI Designer + Arabic RTL Experience Agent to produce:

1. **Sitemap** for both surfaces — every reachable screen.
2. **Screen list** (`dashboard-screen-planner`) per-screen spec: purpose, key components, primary CTA, secondary actions, data shown, permission gate, empty/loading/error variants. Must apply:
   - `wcag-accessibility-checklist` (WCAG 2.2 AA)
   - `i18n-multilang-setup`
   - `rtl-arabic-ui-planner` (RTL mirroring, Arabic-KW copy, locale-aware date/number)
3. **Mobile screens** (Android, RTL-first):
   - Login (employee ID or magic link), Today (status card, big check-in/out button), Check-in flow (geofence check → selfie → QR option → confirmation), History (30 days), Shift (this week + next), Leave (submit + status), Settings, Offline indicator + queue view.
4. **Admin web screens** (LTR + RTL):
   - Login (MFA optional), Dashboard (live attendance board), Employees (CRUD + photo + civil ID + department + manager + shift), Offices (CRUD + geofence map), Shifts (CRUD + grace + break), Live Attendance, Reports (daily/weekly/monthly, CSV + PDF), Leave Management (approve/reject), Audit Log, Settings (roles, feature flags, tenant config).
5. **Arabic-KW copy plan** (`arabic-rtl-experience-agent`) — every screen's primary labels in Arabic-KW + English. Native, not machine-translated.
6. **Mobile layout** described for any screen at ≤375px.
7. **Role visibility** matrix per screen (Super Admin · HR Manager · Dept Manager · Employee).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every screen has empty/loading/error variants
- [ ] Every Arabic-bound screen has RTL variant noted

## App Request — Android Attendance App (تطبيق حضور وانصراف)

**Raw user prompt (Arabic Kuwaiti):**
> طيب ابني لي تطبيق هاتف اندرويد لتسجيل الحضور والانصاراف بشكل متكامل

**Translation:** Build me a complete Android phone app for recording attendance (check-in / check-out).

---

## Classification (auto, via `prompt-router-classifier`)

- **Vertical:** HR / Workforce / Time-Tracking → routed to `hr-system-builder` blueprint (attendance module is the core HR sub-domain).
- **Form factor:** **Mobile-first Android app** (primary) + **Web Admin Dashboard** (secondary, for HR managers to review).
- **Stack:** React Native (Expo) for Android + Next.js admin dashboard + Supabase (Postgres + Auth + RLS + Realtime + Storage) per default tech stack.
- **Language:** Arabic Kuwaiti (default) + English toggle. RTL-first UI.
- **Compliance:** PDPL Kuwait (biometric/location data) + GDPR baseline.
- **Audience:** B2B — companies tracking employee attendance. Multi-tenant.

---

## MVP scope (ship within 7-10 days)

**Employee Android app:**
- Login (employee ID + password or magic link)
- One-tap **Check-In** / **Check-Out** with:
  - GPS geofencing (must be within configured office radius)
  - Optional selfie capture (face match against profile photo)
  - Optional QR scan (office QR code)
- Today's status card (checked in / not yet / hours worked)
- History (last 30 days, with status: on-time / late / absent / leave)
- Shift schedule (this week + next week)
- Leave request (submit + status: pending / approved / rejected)
- Push notifications (shift reminder, missed punch alert, leave decision)
- Offline mode: queue punches when offline, sync when online
- Arabic + English UI, RTL-first

**HR Admin Web Dashboard:**
- Employee directory (CRUD, with photo + civil ID + department + manager + shift assignment)
- Office locations (CRUD with geofence radius)
- Shift templates (CRUD: name, start, end, grace minutes, break)
- Live attendance board (today's check-ins + outs, late list, absent list)
- Reports: daily / weekly / monthly per employee + per department (CSV + PDF export)
- Leave management (approve / reject)
- Audit log (all punches, who changed what)
- Roles: Super Admin · HR Manager · Department Manager · Employee

## Production scope (Phase 2)

- Face recognition for anti-buddy-punching (vs. simple selfie)
- Fingerprint/biometric on-device unlock to authorize a punch
- WhatsApp notifications (in addition to push)
- Multi-shift / split-shift / night-shift handling
- Overtime calculation + payroll export (CSV mapped to common Gulf payroll systems)
- Public holidays calendar (Kuwait) + auto-leave on holidays
- Manager approval workflow (manager approves anomalies: late, missed punch)
- Beacon/BLE check-in alternative (for indoor offices where GPS is weak)
- iOS build (Expo same codebase)
- Integration: Slack / Microsoft Teams for in-channel /attendance commands
- AI insights: late-pattern detection, absence forecasting, anomaly alerts

---

## Default scaffolding (mandatory — every app gets these)

- `multi-tenant-architecture` (workspace_id on every table, RLS-enforced)
- `audit-log-designer` (every check-in / check-out / admin edit logged with actor + ip + device)
- `rate-limiting-design` (punch endpoint: 1 per 30s per employee)
- `i18n-arabic-rtl` (full RTL, Arabic-first labels)
- `wcag-accessibility-checklist` (WCAG 2.2 AA)
- `analytics-posthog` + `sentry-error-tracking` + `web-vitals-budget`
- `notifications-multichannel` (FCM push primary, email fallback)
- `backups-restore-drill` (daily backup, weekly restore drill)
- `pdpl-kuwait-compliance` (biometric data classified sensitive, opt-in consent, civil ID encrypted at rest)
- `feature-flags` (toggle face recognition / selfie / QR per tenant)
- `ci-cd-pipeline` (GitHub Actions → EAS Build for Android APK/AAB + Vercel for web)
- `monitoring-alerting-setup` (uptime + error rate + p95 latency)

---

## Required external scaffolds (will be requested at Architecture Gate, not now)

1. Supabase project (free tier OK for MVP)
2. Vercel project (for admin dashboard)
3. GitHub repo
4. Expo / EAS account (free tier OK for development; paid for production builds)
5. Firebase project (FCM push notifications)
6. Domain (optional for MVP, e.g. `attendance.newtechkw.com`)
7. Google Play Console (production deploy only — Phase 2)

**No paid action will be taken without explicit user approval.**

---

## Acceptance Criteria (parent issue)

- [ ] PRD complete with MVP/Production cut + non-goals + role matrix
- [ ] User stories with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
- [ ] Database schema with RLS policies for multi-tenancy
- [ ] OpenAPI 3.1 contract
- [ ] Sitemap + screen list for both mobile + admin web
- [ ] Build sub-issues created in `05 - Parallel Build` and assigned
- [ ] QA test plan + security review checklist passed
- [ ] Deployment plan + rollback plan documented
- [ ] User guide (Arabic) + Admin guide (Arabic + English)
- [ ] APK/AAB built and signed
- [ ] Admin dashboard deployed (preview URL)

## Definition of Done

System is live, employees can install the Android APK, check in/out within geofence, history syncs, HR admin can review and export reports. Zero exposed secrets. Security review and QA gate passed.

## Risks

- Selfie/face match adds privacy + storage cost — gate behind feature flag, opt-in per tenant.
- GPS spoofing — mitigated by geofence + selfie + WiFi SSID fingerprint (Phase 2: BLE beacons).
- Battery drain from background location — use foreground-only location requests on punch (no background tracking).
- Kuwait PDPL: biometric data is sensitive — requires explicit consent + local encryption.

## Dependencies

None — uses default stack scaffolding.

## Routing

- **Owner squad:** Product UX Squad (intake) → Architecture Squad (schema/API) → Build Squad (parallel build) → QA Security → DevOps Launch.
- **Orchestrator:** `one-prompt-to-app` (owned by `ceo` and `product-manager`).
- **Vertical builder:** `hr-system-builder` (attendance is the core HR module).

# Unbeatable v2 — How the Factory Wins Against Every Competitor

**Status:** Live as of 2026-05-25.
**Supersedes:** none (additive layer on top of [DEV-14] Factory Operating Manual).

This is the architecture that lets one prompt produce a complete, production-grade system without further user input. It is the single source of truth for *what* makes this factory unbeatable vs Lovable / Antigravity / Cursor / Claude-Code-only / v0 / Bolt.

## 1. The unfair advantage in one paragraph

Most AI builders stop at the demo. We don't. **One raw prompt** triggers `one-prompt-to-app`, which auto-classifies the vertical, runs intake → PRD → schema → API → screens → build → QA → deploy in parallel across 8 squads and 33 agents, and never pauses on anything that can be defaulted. Every shipped app inherits 25 non-negotiable defaults (multi-tenant, RLS, audit log, i18n, RTL, WCAG AA, SEO, analytics, error tracking, perf budget, monitoring, backups, rate limits, feature flags, PDPL/GDPR). Failures self-heal across runtimes. A daily health audit and weekly improvement loop keep the factory itself improving non-stop.

## 2. The killer skill: `one-prompt-to-app`

The orchestrator that competitors don't have:

| Step | Skill | Owner |
|------|-------|-------|
| 1 | `prompt-router-classifier` | ceo |
| 2 | `app-idea-to-product-brief` | product-manager |
| 3 | `app-scope-mvp-vs-production` | product-manager |
| 4 | `product-brief-to-prd` | product-manager |
| 5 | `prd-to-user-stories` | product-manager |
| 6 | `auth-permission-matrix` | security-auditor |
| 7 | `database-schema-designer` + `multi-tenant-architecture` + `audit-log-designer` | database-architect |
| 8 | `api-contract-designer` + `api-versioning-strategy` + `rate-limiting-design` | api-architect |
| 9 | `dashboard-screen-planner` + `wcag-accessibility-checklist` + `i18n-multilang-setup` + `rtl-arabic-ui-planner` + `design-system-tokens-builder` | ux-architect / ui-designer |
| 10 | The matching `*-system-builder` (CRM/HR/Delivery/POS/Restaurant/Clinic/School/Real-Estate/Gym/LMS/Marketplace/Email-Marketing/Booking/Maintenance/Inventory) | solution-architect |
| 11 | `user-stories-to-issues` | sprint-commander |
| 12 | Cross-cutting setup (see §3) | full-stack-engineer |
| 13 | Compliance baseline (see §4) | security-auditor |
| 14 | Build via `agentic-codegen-loop` | claude-code-lead-developer |
| 15 | QA gates (see §5) | quality-gate-manager |
| 16 | Launch via `deployment-checklist` + `monitoring-alerting-setup` + `incident-response-runbook` | deployment-engineer |
| 17 | Handover via `documentation-generator` + `admin-guide-generator` + `user-guide-generator` + `api-sdk-generator` | documentation-agent |
| 18 | `reusable-template-extractor` + `post-build-report-generator` + `cross-project-pattern-extractor` | skill-builder / memory-curator |

`one-prompt-to-app` runs all of this without pausing for anything defaultable. Hard blockers (credentials, paid resources, billing model ambiguity) surface as one consolidated user-facing question.

## 3. Cross-cutting defaults (every app gets these, no opt-out)

| Concern | Default skill |
|---------|---------------|
| SEO baseline | `seo-baseline-setup` |
| Product analytics | `analytics-tracking-setup` (PostHog) |
| Error tracking | `error-tracking-setup` (Sentry) |
| Performance budget | `performance-budget-enforcer` (CWV in CI) |
| Realtime collab | `realtime-collaboration-planner` |
| Notifications | `notifications-multichannel-planner` (email + SMS + push + WhatsApp + in-app) |
| Webhooks | `webhooks-framework-planner` |
| Feature flags | `feature-flag-system` |
| A/B testing | `ab-testing-framework` |
| Background jobs | `background-jobs-design` |
| File upload | `file-upload-storage-design` |
| Search | `search-system-builder` + `embeddings-semantic-search` |
| AI chat over data | `ai-chat-with-data` |
| PWA / offline | `pwa-offline-first` |
| Admin console | `admin-console-builder` |
| Onboarding | `onboarding-flow-builder` |
| Subscriptions | `payment-subscription-builder` |
| Realtime dashboards | `realtime-dashboards-builder` |
| Customer support | `chat-support-builder` |
| No-code automations | `workflow-automation-builder` |
| Data export/import | `data-export-import-planner` |
| Data warehouse | `data-warehouse-analytics` |
| KW Civil ID | `kw-civil-id-validator` (when KW market) |
| Seed data | `seed-data-factory` |
| CI/CD | `ci-cd-pipeline-builder` |
| Preview deploys | `live-preview-deploys` |
| Dependency security | `dependency-vulnerability-scanner` |
| SDKs | `api-sdk-generator` |

## 4. Compliance baseline (one of these always applies)

- `pdpl-kuwait-compliance` — every Kuwait-launched app.
- `gdpr-compliance-checklist` — every app with EU users.
- `soc2-readiness-checklist` — every B2B SaaS.

## 5. Quality gates (block-or-pass — no warnings)

Before any `in_review → done`:
- `security-review-checklist` — 15 points.
- `wcag-accessibility-checklist` — AA pass via axe-core CI.
- `performance-review-checklist` + `performance-budget-enforcer` — CWV budget green.
- `dependency-vulnerability-scanner` — no high+ advisory.
- `qa-test-plan-generator` — plan executed, results attached.
- `usability-test-script` — 5 users tested (major features only).
- `agent-output-quality-review` — score ≥ 10/12.

## 6. Self-healing

- `agent-self-healing-policy` — failed run retries on a different runtime (Claude → Codex → Opencode) before bothering anyone.
- `retry_log` metadata captures every attempt for the weekly improvement loop.

## 7. Continuous improvement (non-stop)

Two autopilots run on a schedule:

| Autopilot | Cadence | Owner | Output |
|-----------|---------|-------|--------|
| **Factory Health Daily Audit** | Every day 06:00 Kuwait | workspace-operations-manager | Issue in `00 - Factory Brain` with GREEN/YELLOW/RED status + top 5 findings. |
| **Skill Improvement Weekly Loop** | Every Monday 07:00 Kuwait | skill-builder | Issue in `08 - Skill Library` with top-5 ranked improvements derived from `cross-project-pattern-extractor` + retry_log analysis. |

Both run without user prompting. Silence on the cron is itself a finding.

## 8. The 18 vertical builders (a one-shot for each)

CRM, HR, Delivery, Inventory, Booking, Maintenance, POS, Restaurant, Clinic, School, Real Estate, Gym, LMS, Marketplace, Email Marketing, Shopify dashboard, Zoho automation, WhatsApp automation. Add 19th by writing one `<vertical>-system-builder` skill — the orchestrator picks it up automatically via `prompt-router-classifier`.

## 9. Skill count comparison

| System | Skills | Verticals | Compliance | Self-heal | Continuous improvement |
|--------|--------|-----------|------------|-----------|------------------------|
| Lovable | n/a | none (general) | partial | no | no |
| Antigravity | n/a | none (general) | partial | partial | no |
| Cursor | n/a | none (general) | no | no | no |
| Claude Code only | n/a | none | no | no | no |
| **devosnt App Factory** | **98** | **18** | **PDPL + GDPR + SOC2** | **yes** | **yes (daily + weekly)** |

## 10. What still needs the user

These cannot be done from inside Multica today. The Integration Architect lists exactly which the project needs at Architecture Gate time — never asked unless required.

- Supabase project (https://supabase.com/dashboard/new) — per app.
- Vercel project (`vercel link`) — per app.
- GitHub repo (`gh repo create newtechkw/{slug} --private`) — per app.
- Domain + DNS — per launched app.
- KNet / MyFatoorah / Stripe sandbox — when the app takes money.
- Meta WhatsApp Cloud API — when the app sends WhatsApp.
- Apple Developer + Google Play — when mobile ships to stores.

## 11. How to invoke

- **`build crm`** — Manager CRM end-to-end.
- **`build {anything}`** — any vertical, automatic routing.
- **`audit`** — health audit on demand.
- **Just describe the system in any language** — `one-prompt-to-app` takes it from there.

# Charter — 09 Research Intelligence

## What lives here
Research outputs: survey reports, deep-dives on repos / tools, decision docs (`tool-comparison-workflow`), changelog digests, competitor analyses, best-practice writeups.

## Owning squad
Research Intelligence Squad. Lead: research-scout. Members: github-analyst, docs-analyst, tool-evaluator.

## Inputs accepted
- "Which tool / library should we use for X?" — from Architecture Squad or CEO.
- Weekly changelog sweeps of default stack providers (Supabase, Vercel, Next.js, Expo, Stripe, Meta).
- Competitor benchmarks before launching a new vertical.

## Outputs produced
- Survey report — top N options, ranked, one-line each, recommendation.
- Deep-dive — single repo or tool, code quality, gotchas, integration steps.
- Decision doc — 2-4 options scored against weighted criteria, recommendation + runner-up.
- Changelog digest — what changed in our providers, what (if anything) we must do.

## Status conventions
- `todo` — research-scout picks up; defines the question and the criteria.
- `in_review` — research drafted, awaiting requester's review.
- `done` — research approved + linked back to the consuming project.

## Quality bar
- Every output names sources with links.
- Every recommendation names the rejected alternatives and the reason.
- Decision docs always include the runner-up so the choice can be revisited.
- No vendor marketing copy — extract facts, leave spin.

## Compounding
- Research outputs that drive a decision should be promoted to [08 - Skill Library] so the next project doesn't redo the work.
- Pin `decision`, `runner_up`, `superseded_by` on the research issue.

## When NOT to research
- Architecture already has a confident answer — don't second-guess unless asked.
- User has specified a tool — implement it; only research if it's known to be broken.
- Decision is small enough that an experiment is cheaper than reading.
issues.title: Charter — 09 Research Intelligence

# Charter — 08 Skill Library

## What lives here
Reusable skills, app templates, prompt templates, workflow templates, post-build reports. The workspace's compounding asset — anything that makes the next project faster than the last.

## Owning squad
Skill Factory Squad. Lead: skill-builder. Members: template-builder, prompt-optimizer, memory-curator.

## Inputs accepted
- Triggered by CEO after any project closes (via `reusable-template-extractor`).
- New skill requests from any squad.
- Prompt drift findings from monthly audits.
- The 3-times rule: any workflow seen 3+ times across projects is a candidate.

## Outputs produced
- New Multica skill (via `multica skill create`).
- Reusable starter template (e.g., Next.js + Supabase + RTL boilerplate).
- Prompt edits for existing agents (approved by CEO before going live).
- Quarterly `workspace-upgrade-report`.

## Status conventions
- `todo` — skill-builder picks up; drafts the skill or template.
- `in_review` — drafted, awaiting CEO approval.
- `done` — skill / template merged; announced via comment on the originating project.

## Skill anatomy (every new skill must define)
Name · purpose · trigger · required input · steps · output shape · quality checklist · failure handling · improvement rule.

## When NOT to make a skill
- Pattern seen only once or twice — too early.
- One-off custom work for a single project — keep it project-scoped.
- Workflow that's still volatile — wait for it to stabilize.

# Charter — 07 Deploy Documentation Handover

## What lives here
Deployment plans, environment setup, release notes, rollback plans, user guides, admin guides, handover checklists, post-deploy smoke tests.

## Owning squad
DevOps Launch Squad. Lead: deployment-engineer. Members: environment-manager, release-manager, documentation-agent.

## Inputs accepted
- QA-approved features ready to deploy.
- New environments to provision (dev / staging / prod).
- Documentation requests for shipped apps.

## Outputs produced
- Deployment run — preview verified, prod cutover, post-deploy smoke tests.
- Release notes (Arabic-KW + English for NewTech projects).
- Rollback plan via `rollback-plan-generator`.
- `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `RUNBOOK.md`, `USER_GUIDE.md`, `ADMIN_GUIDE.md`, `ROLLBACK.md` checked into the repo.
- Env Vars Manifest issue updated per [DEV-11].
- `deploy_url`, `release_tag`, `rollback_tested_at` pinned to the issue.

## Status conventions
- `todo` — DevOps agent picks up; runs `deployment-checklist`.
- `in_review` — pre-deploy checklist green, awaiting CEO + QA sign-off.
- `done` — production cutover + post-deploy smoke tests passed.

## Gates owned
- Pre-deploy: 100% green before any production push.
- Post-deploy: smoke tests within 30 min; error rate + p95 within baseline.

## Authority
- Deployment Engineer may roll back unilaterally if error rate > 2× baseline for 5 min.
- All other rollbacks require CEO approval.

## Standards
- DoD: [DEV-3]

issues.description:
# Project Intake Form — Workspace Standard

The first contact between a new project idea and the factory. Every issue created in `01 - App Requests` should answer these questions, either inline or via a linked document. The CEO Orchestrator runs `app-idea-to-product-brief` against the answers to produce the one-page brief.

If the user opens an App Request with just a one-liner ("build me a CRM"), the CEO Orchestrator fills as much as possible from sensible defaults and only asks the user for the blockers — never asks the full questionnaire.

---

## Section 1 — The idea
1. **What does this product do?** One sentence.
2. **Who is it for?** Industry, role, company size.
3. **What problem does it solve today?** What do they do without this product?
4. **Why now?** What changed that makes this worth building?

## Section 2 — Scope
5. **MVP — what's the smallest version that's useful?** Bullet list.
6. **Production — what's the full version after launch?** Bullet list.
7. **Non-goals — what's explicitly NOT in scope?** Bullet list.

## Section 3 — Users & roles
8. **Who logs in?** List every role (admin, manager, staff, customer, …).
9. **What can each role do?** Loose description; the formal role matrix comes later.

## Section 4 — Look & feel
10. **Web, mobile, or both?** If mobile: native app needed, or responsive web is enough?
11. **Arabic-KW UI?** Yes / no / bilingual.
12. **RTL required?** Yes / no.
13. **Brand colors / logo?** Attach if available; otherwise default factory palette.

## Section 5 — Integrations
14. **Payments?** If yes: which provider, KW or global.
15. **Messaging?** WhatsApp / SMS / Email — which channels matter.
16. **Existing tools to integrate with?** Shopify, Zoho, Salesforce, accounting system, etc.
17. **Migrate data from a previous system?** If yes: source + format + volume estimate.

## Section 6 — Constraints
18. **Launch deadline?** Hard or soft.
19. **Budget for paid services?** Per month, approx.
20. **Compliance requirements?** PCI, HIPAA, GDPR, Kuwait DPL, none.
21. **Hosting preference?** Default (Vercel + Supabase) is assumed unless stated.

## Section 7 — Approvals
22. **Who signs off on the PRD?**
23. **Who signs off on launch?**
24. **Who pays for the paid resources (Supabase Pro, Vercel Pro, Stripe fees, etc.)?**

---

## How the CEO Orchestrator handles intake
1. Read the App Request.
2. Run `app-idea-to-product-brief` against whatever is provided.
3. The skill produces a draft brief that fills sensible defaults for unanswered questions (default stack, default roles, default Arabic-KW UI for NewTech).
4. Comment back with the brief + an `Open questions` block listing only the questions where the default could go either way (budget, deadline, specific integrations, compliance).
5. When the user answers the open questions, run `product-brief-to-prd` to produce the PRD in `02 - Product Specs`.

## What NOT to do
- Don't make the user fill all 24 questions. The defaults exist for a reason.
- Don't stop at "we need more info" — fill what you can, ask only the blockers.
- Don't create the PRD before the brief is approved.
- Don't promise dates before the Architecture pack is done.
issues.title: Project Intake Form (use this for every new App Request)

issues.description:
# Factory Operating Manual — Master Index

This is the single entry point for every agent and human working in this workspace. It links every canonical standard, template, and gate that governs the factory.

If you are an agent: **read this manual when triaging any unfamiliar issue.** If a rule isn't here, it doesn't exist yet — add it via a new standard issue, don't invent.

---

## 1. Mission

Build complete production-grade business systems (CRMs, HR, delivery, maintenance, inventory, booking, dashboards, SaaS, mobile apps, automations) end-to-end faster and at higher quality than Lovable, Antigravity, Cursor, or Claude-Code-only workflows. The workspace is the company; the agents are the team.

## 2. Operating loop

**Intake → Plan → Build → Verify → Ship → Compound.** Every request flows through this loop. Skipping a stage is a defect.

## 3. The standards (each is a separate issue — read it before acting)

| Domain | Issue |
|---|---|
| Definition of Done | DEV-3 |
| Quality Gates (Product / Architecture / Release) | DEV-4 |
| Security Rules | DEV-5 |
| Default Tech Stack | DEV-6 |
| Issue Workflow Spec | DEV-7 |
| PRD Template | DEV-8 |
| ADR Template | DEV-9 |
| Permission Matrix Template | DEV-10 |
| Env Vars Manifest | DEV-11 |
| Repo & PR Conventions | DEV-12 |
| Language & Localization Rules | DEV-13 |
| Issue Templates (20) | DEV-2 |

## 4. Squads at a glance

| Squad | Owns | Lead |
|---|---|---|
| CEO Command | Routing, gates, escalation | ceo |
| Product UX | PRD, screens, role matrix | product-manager |
| Architecture | Schema, API, auth, integrations, ADRs | solution-architect |
| Build | Frontend / Backend / Full-stack / Mobile code | claude-code-lead-developer |
| QA Security | Test plans, security, performance, release veto | qa-engineer |
| DevOps Launch | Deploy, env, docs, rollback, handover | deployment-engineer |
| Skill Factory | Skills, templates, prompt drift, memory | skill-builder |
| Research Intelligence | Tool/library research, decision docs | research-scout |

Each squad has its full operating manual in its `instructions` field — `multica squad get <id>` reads it.

## 5. Projects at a glance

| # | Project | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| 00 | Factory Brain | Master rules, standards, ADRs, this manual |
| 01 | App Requests | Intake — every new app starts here |
| 02 | Product Specs | PRDs, user stories, role matrix |
| 03 | UX UI Design System | Sitemap, screens, design tokens, RTL rules |
| 04 | Architecture Database API | Schema, API, auth, integrations |
| 05 | Parallel Build | Frontend / Backend / Mobile coding tasks |
| 06 | QA Security Performance | Test plans, bugs, security, perf |
| 07 | Deploy Documentation Handover | Deploy, docs, runbooks |
| 08 | Skill Library | Reusable skills, templates |
| 09 | Research Intelligence | Tool research, decision docs |

## 6. The IDEA → DEPLOYED SYSTEM workflow

```
User idea
   ↓
[01 - App Requests]  ceo runs app-idea-to-product-brief
   ↓
[02 - Product Specs] product-manager runs product-brief-to-prd → prd-to-user-stories
   ↓                          (in parallel with)
[03 - UX UI]         ux-architect runs dashboard-screen-planner + rtl-arabic-ui-planner
[04 - Architecture]  architecture squad runs database-schema-designer + api-contract-designer + auth-permission-matrix
   ↓
   CEO promotes [05 - Parallel Build] children from backlog → todo
   ↓
[05 - Parallel Build] Build Squad implements; one PR per feature
   ↓
[06 - QA Security]   QA Squad runs full pass + security-review-checklist + performance-review-checklist
   ↓
[07 - Deploy]        DevOps Squad runs deployment-checklist + rollback-plan-generator + docs
   ↓
[08 - Skill Library] Skill Factory runs reusable-template-extractor → next project starts faster
```

## 7. Gates (none are optional)

- **Product Gate** — owned by Product UX. PRD complete, role matrix locked, screen list with empty/loading/error per screen.
- **Architecture Gate** — owned by Architecture. Schema + RLS + API + auth + integrations + cost estimate.
- **Release Gate** — owned by QA Security. 15-point block list (see DEV-4).

Only the CEO Orchestrator may override a gate veto, and must comment the reason + accepted risk + revisit date.

## 8. When to escalate

- To the CEO Command Squad: scope changes after PRD lock, cross-squad disputes, security findings, deploy/paid-resource asks, effort 2× plan.
- To the workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com): credentials, paid resources, production deploys, missing Multica capability, unresolvable CEO disputes.

## 9. Language

- Technical work / issues / code / PRs / commits: **English**.
- NewTech customer-facing copy: **Arabic Kuwaiti** unless told otherwise.
- See DEV-13 for full rules.

## 10. How to use this manual

- **New issue in your inbox?** Open the issue → check the project → re-read the relevant standard → act.
- **Unclear what's expected?** Read this manual top-to-bottom; the answer is usually one of the 11 standards.
- **A standard contradicts itself or is missing something?** File a new standard issue in 00 - Factory Brain; don't improvise.
- **A workflow happened 3+ times the same way?** File a skill-extraction issue in 08 - Skill Library.

## 11. Versioning

- This manual is updated by the workspace-operations-manager when a standard changes.
- ADRs that change a default stack choice supersede the relevant section.
- The CEO Orchestrator announces material changes in this issue's comments.

---

_Last bootstrapped: 2026-05-25 by ceo._
