# Language & Localization Rules — Workspace Standard

## Internal technical work — always English
- Code, identifiers, comments, file names.
- Issue titles, descriptions, comments.
- PRDs, ADRs, runbooks, schemas, API docs.
- PR titles + bodies.
- Commit messages.

Why: agents, search, and tools work best in English. Mixing languages in tickets makes them un-greppable.

## Customer-facing — Arabic Kuwaiti by default for NewTech work
Unless the user specifies otherwise, NewTech customer-facing copy is **Arabic, Kuwaiti register**:
- App UI strings.
- Email & WhatsApp templates.
- Marketing pages.
- User guide + admin guide.
- Release notes.

## Bilingual where it helps
- User and admin guides may be bilingual when handed to external developers or non-Arabic admins.
- Error messages: tech detail in English (for logs), user-facing message in the user's locale.

## RTL — when Arabic is in scope, RTL is in scope
- Mirror layout (sidebars, nav, icons that imply direction).
- Tailwind: use the `dir` plugin + logical properties (`ps-*`, `pe-*`, `me-*`, `ms-*`) over `pl-*`/`pr-*`.
- Inline icons that imply direction (←, →, chevrons): flip via `[dir="rtl"]:rotate-180`.
- Numerals: keep Western digits (0–9) by default; switch to Eastern Arabic only when the user requests.
- Dates: locale-aware; for Arabic-KW use Gregorian + Arabic month names.
- Currency: `KWD` displayed as `د.ك` in customer-facing UI.

## Arabic-KW register notes
- Use Kuwaiti dialect for SMS / WhatsApp / marketing — sounds natural, not stiff.
- Use Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) for legal text, T&Cs, formal admin documentation.
- Avoid auto-translated MSA; it reads robotic to Kuwaiti users.

## Owner agents
- **arabic-rtl-experience-agent** (Product UX Squad) — owns Arabic copy + RTL rules per project.
- Reviews every customer-facing string before launch.

## Localization tooling
- **next-intl** for Next.js apps. Locale files in `messages/{en,ar-KW}.json`.
- Keys are dot-paths in English: `leads.list.empty.title`.
- Never inline user-facing strings in code; always reference the message key.

## Validation
- QA test plan includes RTL screenshots for every screen on Arabic-bound projects.
- A native Arabic-KW reviewer signs off marketing & customer-facing copy before launch (or the workspace owner does).

## When in doubt
- If the audience is engineers anywhere: English.
- If the audience is a NewTech end user / customer: Arabic-KW.
- If the audience is the workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com): either is fine; default Arabic-KW for greetings, English for technical body.
issues.title: Standard: Language & Localization Rules

issues.description:
# Repo & PR Conventions — Workspace Standard

## Repo layout (per project)
```
{project-name}/
├── web/                     # Next.js app
│   ├── app/                 # App Router
│   ├── components/
│   ├── lib/
│   ├── public/
│   └── tests/
├── mobile/                  # Expo app (only if mobile is in scope)
│   ├── app/
│   ├── components/
│   └── lib/
├── supabase/
│   ├── migrations/          # Forward-only, timestamped
│   ├── seed.sql
│   ├── functions/           # Edge functions
│   └── policies/            # RLS policies as separate review unit
├── docs/
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── ARCHITECTURE.md
│   ├── RUNBOOK.md
│   ├── USER_GUIDE.md        # bilingual if Arabic-bound
│   ├── ADMIN_GUIDE.md       # bilingual if Arabic-bound
│   ├── ROLLBACK.md
│   └── adr/                 # ADR-001 … ADR-NNN
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/           # CI: typecheck, lint, test, secret-scan, deploy
├── .env.example             # Lists every var with a placeholder; never real values
├── package.json
└── pnpm-workspace.yaml
```

## Branches
- `main` — always green, always deployable. Production deploys cut from here.
- Feature branches: `feat/{issue-id}-short-slug`.
- Bug branches: `fix/{issue-id}-short-slug`.
- No long-lived dev / develop branches. Trunk-based.

## Commits — Conventional Commits
```
type(scope): subject

[body]
[footer]
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `style`, `build`, `ci`.
Scope is optional but encouraged (e.g., `feat(leads): add bulk assign`).

## PRs
**Title:** mirror the lead commit; keep under 70 chars.

**Body template:**
```
## Summary
- bullet 1
- bullet 2

## Why
[link to issue + one paragraph]

## Screenshots
[required for any UI change]

## Test plan
- [ ] Step 1
- [ ] Step 2

## Env vars
[any new or changed]

## Migrations
[any new migrations + the rollback path]

## Risks
[anything reviewers should look at hardest]

Closes #{issue-id}
```

**Rules:**
- One feature per PR. No drive-by refactors. A refactor needs its own PR.
- All checks green before merge: typecheck, lint, test, secret-scan, RLS policy test (if schema changed).
- At least one review by another agent (or a human for production-impacting changes).
- Squash and merge. The squash commit becomes the changelog entry.
- Never `git push --no-verify`. If a hook fails, fix the underlying issue.
- Never `git commit --amend` to a published commit.
- Never force-push to `main`. Force-push to a feature branch is fine before review.

## Tags & releases
- Production deploys cut a tag: `v{MAJOR}.{MINOR}.{PATCH}` (semver).
- `MAJOR` — breaking change.
- `MINOR` — new feature, backwards-compatible.
- `PATCH` — bugfix, no API change.
- Release notes generated from the squash commits since the previous tag.

## CI baseline (every project)
- `typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit`.
- `lint` — `eslint` + `prettier --check`.
- `test` — `vitest run` or `jest`.
- `secret-scan` — `gitleaks` or equivalent.
- `rls-test` — runs the RLS policy test suite against a fresh Postgres.
- `build` — `next build` (catches build-time errors).

## When CI is red
- The branch cannot merge. Fix the root cause; don't skip checks.
- If the check itself is broken (false positive), that's its own bug — file an issue, fix in a separate PR.
issues.title: Standard: Repo & PR Conventions

issues.description:
# Env Vars Manifest — Workspace Standard

Every project maintains a single env manifest issue listing every variable it consumes. The Environment Manager (DevOps Launch Squad) owns this issue. No deploy lands until this manifest is current.

## Manifest shape

For each env var:

| Var | Required in | Scope | Source | Rotation policy | Owner | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | dev, staging, prod | client | Supabase dashboard | — | env-manager | Public; safe in client bundle |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | dev, staging, prod | client | Supabase dashboard | annual | env-manager | Public anon key |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | server only | server | Supabase dashboard | quarterly | env-manager | **NEVER** in client |
| `DATABASE_URL` | server only | server | Supabase / Vercel Postgres | quarterly | env-manager | Migrations + edge functions |
| `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` | server only | server | Stripe dashboard | quarterly | env-manager | If project takes payments |
| `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | server only | server | Stripe webhook config | per-endpoint | env-manager | One per webhook URL |
| `MYFATOORAH_API_KEY` | server only | server | MyFatoorah portal | quarterly | env-manager | KW payments |
| `META_WHATSAPP_TOKEN` | server only | server | Meta Business | 60 days | env-manager | Long-lived token |
| `META_WHATSAPP_PHONE_ID` | server only | server | Meta Business | — | env-manager | — |
| `RESEND_API_KEY` | server only | server | Resend dashboard | annual | env-manager | Email |
| `SENTRY_DSN` | dev, staging, prod | both | Sentry | — | env-manager | Error tracking |
| `POSTHOG_KEY` | dev, staging, prod | client | PostHog | — | env-manager | Product analytics |
| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` | server only | server | Generated | annual | env-manager | If using Auth.js |

## Rules
1. Public vars (`NEXT_PUBLIC_*`) are safe in the client bundle. Everything else is server-only — if you find it in the browser, that's an S1 security bug.
2. Every var listed here must exist in **every** environment it's marked required in. CI fails if any required var is missing.
3. Rotation: when a key is rotated, pin `rotated_at` to this manifest issue with the date and the new key's last 4 chars.
4. When a key is exposed (accidentally committed, leaked in a screenshot, etc.): rotate immediately and post-mortem within 24h.
5. Service-role keys never go in `.env.local` files that get committed. Use the host's secret store (Vercel env, Supabase config).

## Where secrets live
- **Vercel project env** — for Next.js apps. Set per env (Production / Preview / Development).
- **Supabase dashboard** — for Supabase-side secrets (Edge Function vars).
- **GitHub Actions secrets** — only if CI needs them (deploy keys, npm tokens).
- **1Password / Bitwarden** — for human-only secrets (dashboard logins).

## Required before first deploy
- [ ] Manifest issue created and current.
- [ ] All vars set in target env.
- [ ] Sentry DSN active.
- [ ] Rotation calendar entries created for every secret with a rotation policy.
issues.title: Standard: Env Vars Manifest

issues.description:
# Permission Matrix Template — Workspace Standard

The Permission Matrix is the contract that Architecture's RLS policies and Frontend's route guards both implement. It must exist before any user-scoped table is built.

## Output of the `auth-permission-matrix` skill

For every project:

### 1. Role definitions
| Role | Description | Auth method | Typical user |
|---|---|---|---|
| `admin` | Full control. Can grant/revoke any role. | Email + MFA | NewTech owner |
| `manager` | Manages a team. Can see team data; can't touch other teams. | Email | Department head |
| `staff` | Day-to-day operator. Sees their own work + their team's where allowed. | Email | Sales rep, agent |
| `customer` | End user. Sees only their own data. | Email or social | Buyer / patient / tenant |
| `service` | Server-to-server. No human session. | API key | Webhook receiver, cron job |

### 2. Resource × Action matrix
For every domain resource (Lead, Deal, Order, Invoice, …), define:

| Resource | admin | manager | staff | customer | service |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lead.create | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (own) | ✗ | ✓ |
| Lead.read | ✓ | ✓ (team) | ✓ (own + team-shared) | ✗ | ✓ |
| Lead.update | ✓ | ✓ (team) | ✓ (own) | ✗ | ✓ |
| Lead.delete | ✓ | ✓ (team) | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Lead.assign | ✓ | ✓ (team) | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Lead.export | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |

Scope qualifiers:
- `own` — actor is the row's owner.
- `team` — actor's `team_id` matches the row's `team_id`.
- `team-shared` — actor's team has the row shared via an explicit ACL row.
- `all` — no scope restriction.

### 3. RLS policy mapping
For each row in the matrix, name the Postgres RLS policy that enforces it. Architecture must implement; QA must verify.

| Matrix row | Policy name | Predicate |
|---|---|---|
| Lead.read by staff | `lead_read_staff` | `auth.uid() = owner_id OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM lead_share WHERE lead_id = id AND team_id = (SELECT team_id FROM profile WHERE id = auth.uid()))` |
| … | … | … |

### 4. Route guard mapping
For each route in the Next.js app, name the role(s) that may access it. UI guard is a UX optimization; DB enforces.

| Route | Allowed roles | Server check | Client guard |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/admin/*` | `admin` | `requireRole('admin')` middleware | `<RoleGate role="admin">` |
| `/manager/*` | `admin`, `manager` | … | … |

### 5. Audit log entries
List every action that must be audited:
- Role grant / revoke.
- Permission override.
- Delete / hard-delete.
- Payment / refund.
- Export of customer data.

### 6. QA verification plan
For each Resource × Action row, QA must:
- Sign in as the role.
- Attempt the action both within and outside the documented scope.
- Verify allowed actions succeed and disallowed ones return 403 (not 404, not 500, not "empty list").
- Confirm the audit log row was written where required.

### 7. Sign-off
- [ ] product-manager (matrix is correct)
- [ ] database-architect (RLS policies exist)
- [ ] api-architect (route guards exist)
- [ ] security-auditor (matrix tested end-to-end)

issues.description:
# Issue Workflow Spec — Workspace Standard

How issues move through the factory, who is allowed to move them, and what "ready" means at each step.

## Statuses
- `backlog` — wait. Assignee is set but the agent does not fire yet. Used for serial dependencies.
- `todo` — start now. Triggers the assignee.
- `in_progress` — assignee is actively working. Set automatically when an agent picks up `todo`.
- `in_review` — work submitted, awaiting QA / CEO / Quality Gate.
- `done` — DoD met, shipped, closed.
- `blocked` — paused. The blocker is the most recent comment.
- `cancelled` — abandoned with reason in the closing comment.

## Status transitions and who may make them

| From → To | Allowed actor |
|---|---|
| `backlog → todo` | CEO Orchestrator (after upstream input lands) |
| `todo → in_progress` | The assigned agent on pickup |
| `in_progress → in_review` | The assigned agent when work is ready for review |
| `in_review → done` | QA Security Squad (after gate passes), CEO Command Squad (final close) |
| `in_review → todo` | QA Security Squad (gate failed) — comment must name the failing rule |
| any → `blocked` | Any agent, with a comment naming the blocker |
| `blocked → todo` | The squad that resolved the blocker, with a comment naming the resolution |
| any → `cancelled` | CEO Command Squad only |

## Parent / sub-issue rules
- Parallel children: all created with `--status todo`.
- Strict-serial children: only the first is `todo`; the rest are `--status backlog`. CEO promotes them in turn.
- A parent stays open until all children are `in_review` or `done`.
- Only the CEO Command Squad may close a parent with open children, and must comment the reason.

## Routing rules (where new issues land)
- New app idea → `01 - App Requests`. Assignee = ceo. Status = `todo`.
- PRD work → `02 - Product Specs`. Assignee = product-manager.
- UX / screen work → `03 - UX UI Design System`. Assignee = ux-architect.
- Schema / API / auth → `04 - Architecture Database API`. Assignee = solution-architect.
- Coding tasks → `05 - Parallel Build`. Assignee depends on layer (frontend, backend, full-stack, mobile).
- Test plans / bugs / security review / perf → `06 - QA Security Performance`. Assignee = qa-engineer.
- Deploy / docs / handover → `07 - Deploy Documentation Handover`. Assignee = deployment-engineer.
- Skill / template extraction → `08 - Skill Library`. Assignee = skill-builder.
- Research / tool eval → `09 - Research Intelligence`. Assignee = research-scout.

## Required fields on every issue
Issues missing any of these are returned to the creator:
- Title.
- Owner squad + owner agent.
- Priority (`urgent`, `high`, `medium`, `low`).
- Input (what the assignee needs to start).
- Expected Output (what "done" produces).
- Acceptance Criteria.
- Dependencies (other issues, external).
- Risk.
- Definition of Done (link to the workspace DoD or list the per-issue exceptions).

## Labels — taxonomy
Use the workspace's existing labels. Multiple labels per issue is fine.
- **Layer:** `product`, `ux-ui`, `architecture`, `database`, `api`, `backend`, `frontend`, `mobile`, `integration`.
- **Function:** `qa`, `security`, `performance`, `deploy`, `docs`, `research`, `skill`.
- **Phase:** `intake`, `mvp`, `production`.
- **Type:** `bug`, `rtl-ar`.

## SLAs (default — projects may set tighter)
- Triage (new issue → assignee starts): 4 working hours.
- Gate review (in_review → verdict): 1 working day.
- Bug S1 (critical / customer-facing): same day.
- Bug S2: 2 working days.
- Bug S3 / S4: next sprint.

## Metadata conventions
Only pin facts future runs will re-read:
- `pr_url`, `pr_number`
- `pipeline_status`
- `deploy_url`
- `external_issue_url`
- `waiting_on`, `blocked_reason`
- `decision`
- Project-specific: `gate_override_reason`, `gate_override_revisit_by`, `rotated_at`, `qa_status`, `release_tag`

Never pin: run state, attempts, agent IDs, run timestamps, single-run details, secrets.
issues.title: Standard: Issue Workflow Spec

issues.description:
# Default Tech Stack — Workspace Standard

Use this stack unless the project genuinely doesn't fit. Deviation requires CEO approval and an ADR with rejected alternatives.

## Web frontend
- **Framework:** Next.js (App Router) on TypeScript.
- **UI:** Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui components.
- **Forms:** react-hook-form + Zod for validation.
- **Data fetching:** React Server Components first; TanStack Query when client-side needed.
- **State:** URL params + server state first; Zustand only for genuinely client-only state.
- **i18n:** next-intl. Default locale `en`; add `ar-KW` for NewTech projects.
- **RTL:** Tailwind `dir` plugin + locale-aware components.

## Backend / API
- **Default:** Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions).
- **When Supabase doesn't fit:** Node.js + TypeScript + Fastify, or Next.js API routes for tightly coupled apps.
- **API style:** REST + OpenAPI 3.1. GraphQL only when the front-end justifies it.
- **Validation:** Zod, both directions.
- **Jobs / cron:** Supabase scheduled functions or Vercel cron. Inngest if workflow is complex.

## Database
- **Engine:** Postgres (Supabase-managed by default).
- **Migrations:** Supabase CLI migrations, checked into the repo.
- **Policies:** RLS on every user-scoped table — no exceptions.
- **Naming:** snake_case tables, columns, foreign keys.
- **Timestamps:** `created_at`, `updated_at` (both `timestamptz`).
- **Soft delete:** `deleted_at timestamptz` when destroying data is risky; otherwise hard delete.

## Auth
- **Provider:** Supabase Auth.
- **Methods:** email + password by default; OAuth (Google) when the project needs it.
- **Sessions:** 1-hour access token, 30-day refresh, refresh rotation on.
- **MFA:** required for `admin` role in any project handling money or PII.

## Mobile
- **Framework:** React Native + Expo (managed workflow).
- **Navigation:** Expo Router (file-based, mirrors Next.js).
- **Build:** EAS Build.
- **Push:** Expo Push.

## Payments
- **Kuwait:** KNet (via MyFatoorah aggregator).
- **Global:** Stripe.
- **Subscriptions:** Stripe Billing.
- **Always:** webhook-driven state, idempotency keys, reconciliation job.

## Messaging / Integrations
- **WhatsApp:** Meta WhatsApp Cloud API. Templates pre-approved.
- **SMS:** Twilio (global) or local KW provider when required.
- **Email:** Resend (transactional) or SendGrid (high volume).
- **Files:** Supabase Storage. Signed URLs, time-limited.

## Deployment
- **Web:** Vercel — preview per PR, production on `main` merge.
- **Database:** Supabase managed.
- **Mobile:** Expo EAS → Apple TestFlight → App Store / Google Play Internal → Production.
- **Containers:** Docker only when needed (long-running background services).

## Observability
- **Errors:** Sentry (web + mobile + backend).
- **Logs:** Vercel built-in + Supabase logs.
- **Analytics:** PostHog (product analytics) — self-hosted or cloud.
- **Uptime:** Better Stack or BetterUptime.

## AI / Tooling
- **Multica** — agents, squads, issues, control plane.
- **Hermes** — long-horizon planning, memory, skill execution.
- **Claude Code** — coding, repo edits, refactors, tests.
- **Codex** — alternative coder for parallel build.
- **Opencode / Openclaw** — diversity / cost balancing.
- **MCP** — only when it adds real integration value, never as decoration.

## Repo conventions
- Monorepo per app: `web/`, `mobile/`, `supabase/` (migrations + policies).
- `pnpm` as package manager.
- Conventional Commits.
- One PR per feature; no drive-by refactors.

## Cost defaults
- Supabase Free → Pro at 50k MAU or when backups need PITR.
- Vercel Hobby → Pro before any production launch.
- Domain via Cloudflare (cheaper + better DNS).

## When to deviate
- The user explicitly chose a different stack.
- A core integration requires a different runtime (e.g., Python ML model).
- The default stack genuinely can't meet the requirements (rare).
- Document the deviation as an ADR; CEO + Architecture squad sign off.
issues.title: Standard: Default Tech Stack

issues.description:
# Security Rules — Workspace Standard

These rules are non-negotiable. Violating any one of them blocks ship.

## Secrets
- Never commit, log, paste in comments, or attach files containing API keys, tokens, passwords, OAuth client secrets, database URLs with credentials, service-role keys, or signed URLs that don't expire.
- Use environment variables in every deploy environment. Capture them in the project's env manifest issue.
- Rotate any secret that has been exposed — even if the exposure was brief. Pin `rotated_at` on the secret's environment issue.
- Pre-commit scans: every PR must pass a secret scan (`*.env`, `credentials.*`, `*_key`, `password`, `token`, `private_key`).

## Authentication
- Auth is never optional. Every app ships with role-based permissions and a documented role matrix.
- Use the platform's first-party auth (Supabase Auth or Auth.js). Do not roll custom auth.
- Sessions must expire. Refresh tokens must rotate. No infinite sessions.
- MFA is required for admin accounts on any production system handling money or PII.

## Authorization (RLS-first)
- Every user-scoped table has Row-Level-Security policies.
- The UI-level permission check is a UX optimization, not a security boundary. The DB enforces.
- Policies are reviewed by `security-auditor` before the table goes to production.
- Tests must exercise the policy: a user in role X must fail to read a row scoped to role Y.

## Input validation
- Every API endpoint validates input with a typed schema (Zod / Valibot) server-side.
- Validation rejects (returns 400) — it does not silently coerce.
- Output is typed; no `any` leaks across the API boundary.

## Output sanitization
- HTML never rendered from untrusted strings without sanitization.
- File uploads are stored outside the web root and served via signed, time-limited URLs.
- Error messages to the client never include stack traces, query strings, or internal IDs.

## PII handling
- Per project, maintain a PII inventory: which tables / columns hold PII, what type, who has access.
- Retention policy stated per PII column.
- Logs never include PII unredacted. User identifiers may be logged as opaque IDs.
- Right-to-be-forgotten path documented for any project storing customer PII.

## Audit logging
- Every destructive action (delete, role change, payment, refund, permission grant) writes an audit log row.
- Audit rows include: actor, target, before-state, after-state, timestamp, IP/user-agent if web.
- Audit logs are append-only. Never deletable from the app surface.

## Approvals required
- Production deploys: CEO + workspace owner.
- Paid resource provisioning: workspace owner.
- Mass emails / WhatsApp blasts: workspace owner.
- Destructive ops on production data (truncate, drop, mass update without WHERE): workspace owner with the rollback path attested.
- Granting admin role to a new account: CEO + workspace owner.

## Forbidden by default
- `curl` / `wget` to Multica resources — always use the `multica` CLI.
- `git push --no-verify`.
- `git commit --amend` to published commits.
- `git reset --hard` on shared branches.
- Dropping a production table or column.
- Disabling RLS on a table that has it.
- Logging full request bodies on auth endpoints.
- Storing payment card numbers anywhere outside a PCI-DSS-compliant provider.

## Required before any project is "ready to start"
- Provider list (Supabase, Vercel, GitHub, Stripe/KNet/MyFatoorah, Meta if applicable).
- Secrets storage decision (Vercel env, Supabase dashboard, GitHub secrets).
- Backup strategy (Supabase daily backups confirmed enabled).
- Incident response contact (CEO + workspace owner email).
issues.title: Standard: Security Rules

issues.description:
# Quality Gates — Workspace Standard

Three gates sit between an idea and a shipped feature. None of them are optional. The squad that owns each gate has veto power; the CEO can override but must comment the reason and accept the risk in writing.

---

## Gate 1 — Product Gate (owned by Product UX Squad)

Triggers when an issue moves from `01 - App Requests` → `02 - Product Specs`.

**Blocks if any of these are missing:**
1. Business goal stated in one sentence.
2. Named user personas with top 3 jobs-to-be-done each.
3. MVP scope explicit + Production scope explicit + non-goals.
4. Role matrix (who can see / do what).
5. Top 10 user stories with acceptance criteria.
6. Screen list with empty/loading/error states called out per screen.
7. Localization decision (English-only / Arabic-only / bilingual + RTL).
8. Open questions block — anything that requires the user before Architecture starts.

**Sign-off:** product-manager + ux-architect.

---

## Gate 2 — Architecture Gate (owned by Architecture Squad)

Triggers when an issue moves from `02 - Product Specs` → `05 - Parallel Build`.

**Blocks if any of these are missing:**
1. ADR-001 — stack choice with rejected alternatives.
2. ER diagram + Postgres DDL + RLS policies per table.
3. OpenAPI 3.1 doc with every endpoint, request/response shape, status codes, error model.
4. Auth model — provider, token flow, session, refresh, RBAC wired to the role matrix.
5. Integration briefs — per-integration: purpose, auth, rate limits, failure mode, idempotency.
6. Security model — secret storage, PII inventory, retention, audit log requirement.
7. Cost estimate at 100, 1k, 10k users.
8. Env var list.

**Sign-off:** solution-architect + database-architect + api-architect.

---

## Gate 3 — Release Gate (owned by QA Security Squad)

Triggers when an issue moves from `in_review` → `done`.

**Blocks if ANY one of these is true:**
1. RLS missing or misconfigured on a user-scoped table.
2. Secrets visible in client bundle / comment / commit / log.
3. Auth bypass possible (direct DB call without policy check).
4. Cross-role data leak (role X sees data scoped to role Y).
5. Form accepts invalid input that the server then accepts.
6. Loading state missing on any async UI.
7. Empty state missing on any list.
8. Error state missing on any failed call.
9. Mobile breakpoint broken at ≤ 375px.
10. RTL broken on any Arabic-bound screen.
11. Lighthouse score < 80 on first-page-load for a customer-facing route.
12. p95 query > 500ms on a hot path without an index.
13. No rollback plan attached.
14. No documented deployment steps.
15. Audit log missing for any destructive action.

**Sign-off:** security-auditor (veto right) + qa-engineer + performance-tester.

---

## Override authority

Only the CEO Orchestrator may override a gate veto.
Override requires a comment with:
- Which gate was vetoed.
- Why the override is acceptable.
- The risk being accepted.
- The owner who will revisit / close the risk by a stated date.

Pin `gate_override_reason` and `gate_override_revisit_by` to the issue metadata.
issues.title: Standard: Quality Gates (Product / Architecture / Release)

issues.description:
# Definition of Done — Workspace Standard

A task is "done" only when ALL of the following are true. Anything less goes back to `todo`.

## Universal DoD (every issue)
- [ ] Output complete and matches the issue's stated Acceptance Criteria.
- [ ] All risks named in the issue have a documented mitigation or accepted-with-reason note.
- [ ] The next concrete step (or "no follow-up needed") is stated in the closing comment.
- [ ] No secrets, tokens, or credentials in code, logs, comments, or attachments.
- [ ] Documentation touched by the change is updated in the same PR / change set.
- [ ] If this workflow has now happened 3+ times, a skill-extraction issue is filed in `08 - Skill Library`.

## Code DoD (every PR)
- [ ] TypeScript: compiles in strict mode.
- [ ] Lint + format pass.
- [ ] Tests added or updated for the new behavior.
- [ ] Test suite green locally and in CI.
- [ ] Schema migrations are forward-only and reversible (or the reverse path is documented).
- [ ] RLS policies present for every new user-scoped table.
- [ ] No `console.log` / debug code left in.
- [ ] PR description: what + why + screenshots (if UI) + test plan + env var changes.

## UI DoD (every user-facing screen)
- [ ] Empty state present.
- [ ] Loading state present.
- [ ] Error state present.
- [ ] Form validation client-side + server-side.
- [ ] Keyboard accessible (tab order works, Enter submits, Esc cancels modals).
- [ ] Mobile responsive at 375px width.
- [ ] RTL variant for Arabic-bound screens.
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA on text.
- [ ] No layout shift after data loads.

## Security DoD (every shippable feature)
- [ ] `security-review-checklist` skill executed and passed.
- [ ] Auth required on every protected route.
- [ ] Permissions enforced at the DB layer (RLS), not just the UI.
- [ ] User input validated server-side with a typed schema (Zod or equivalent).
- [ ] No PII in logs.
- [ ] Audit log entry for every destructive action.

## Launch DoD (every production deploy)
- [ ] Pre-deploy checklist 100% green.
- [ ] Post-deploy smoke tests executed within 30 minutes.
- [ ] Rollback path tested in staging within the last 7 days.
- [ ] Release notes published (Arabic-KW + English for NewTech projects).
- [ ] User guide + admin guide updated.
- [ ] On-call agent / human identified.

## Sign-off authority
- Code DoD: Build Squad lead (claude-code-lead-developer).
- UI DoD: Product UX Squad (ui-designer) + QA Engineer.
- Security DoD: QA Security Squad (security-auditor). **Veto right.**
- Launch DoD: DevOps Launch Squad (deployment-engineer) + CEO Command Squad sign-off.
- Universal DoD: CEO Orchestrator final check before closing.
issues.title: Standard: Definition of Done

issues.description:
# Issue Template Library — devosnt App Factory

Twenty copy-paste templates that match the workspace Issue Rules (Title, Owner Squad, Owner Agent, Priority, Input, Expected Output, Acceptance Criteria, Dependencies, Risk, Definition of Done).

When you start a new issue, find the matching template, copy from `---` to `---`, and paste into the description. Replace `{{placeholders}}`.

> Tip: status defaults — start now = `--status todo`, wait for upstream = `--status backlog`.

---

## 1. New App Request

```
**Title:** [APP] {{app_name}} — initial request
**Project:** 01 - App Requests
**Owner squad:** Product UX Squad
**Owner agent:** product-manager
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- One-paragraph description of the app
- Target audience
- Business goal
- Hard constraints (deadline, budget, must integrate with X)

**Expected output**
- Product brief (output of `app-idea-to-product-brief`)
- MVP/Production scope split (output of `app-scope-mvp-vs-production`)
- A sub-issue created in `02 - Product Specs` for PRD

**Acceptance criteria**
- Brief covers: problem, users, primary value, success metric, non-goals.
- MVP scope is shippable in ≤ 2 weeks.

**Dependencies:** none

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Brief approved by user (comment or 6h silent acceptance)
- Sub-issue handed off to product-manager in `02 - Product Specs`
```

---

## 2. PRD Creation

```
**Title:** [PRD] {{app_name}}
**Project:** 02 - Product Specs
**Owner squad:** Product UX Squad
**Owner agent:** product-manager
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Approved product brief (link)
- MVP/Production scope (link)

**Expected output**
- PRD covering problem, users, scope, roles, journeys, non-goals, success metrics
- User stories with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
- Role × action permission matrix (output of `auth-permission-matrix`)

**Acceptance criteria**
- Every user story maps to at least one screen.
- Non-Goals section exists and is explicit.
- Roles cover at least Admin + primary user role.

**Dependencies:** Brief approved

**Risk:** medium — scope creep

**Definition of Done**
- PRD reviewed by CEO Orchestrator
- Sub-issues created in `03 - UX UI Design System` and `04 - Architecture Database API`
```

---

## 3. UX / UI Planning

```
**Title:** [UX] {{app_name}} — sitemap + screens
**Project:** 03 - UX UI Design System
**Owner squad:** Product UX Squad
**Owner agent:** ux-architect
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- PRD (link)
- Permission matrix (link)

**Expected output**
- Sitemap (mermaid)
- Screen list with per-screen spec: purpose, primary action, secondary actions, role visibility
- Empty/loading/error states per screen
- Mobile breakpoint notes per screen
- RTL plan if Arabic audience (sub-issue to arabic-rtl-experience-agent)

**Acceptance criteria**
- Every user story has at least one matching screen.
- Depth ≤ 3 clicks from dashboard to any primary action.

**Dependencies:** PRD approved

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Screen list approved
- UI Designer assigned to compose screens
```

---

## 4. Database Design

```
**Title:** [DB] {{app_name}} — schema + RLS
**Project:** 04 - Architecture Database API
**Owner squad:** Architecture Squad
**Owner agent:** database-architect
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- PRD (link)
- Permission matrix (link)

**Expected output**
- `schema.sql` (DDL)
- `erd.md` (mermaid)
- `rls-policies.sql` — one block per user-scoped table
- Indexes for every FK + common filter
- `audit_log` for sensitive entities

**Acceptance criteria**
- RLS enabled on every user-scoped table.
- Anon query against any user-scoped table fails.
- Money = numeric(18,4); timestamps = timestamptz.

**Dependencies:** PRD approved

**Risk:** high — schema mistakes compound

**Definition of Done**
- Migration runs clean on a fresh Supabase project.
- Smoke test confirms RLS as anon and as authenticated.
```

---

## 5. API Design

```
**Title:** [API] {{app_name}} — OpenAPI 3.1 contract
**Project:** 04 - Architecture Database API
**Owner squad:** Architecture Squad
**Owner agent:** api-architect
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- PRD + user stories
- Schema

**Expected output**
- Complete OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- Error shape standard (`{code, message, details?}`)
- Pagination convention (cursor)
- Idempotency convention (Idempotency-Key)

**Acceptance criteria**
- Every user story has ≥ 1 endpoint.
- Every endpoint has request + 200 + error examples.

**Dependencies:** Schema approved

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- Spec passes `openapi-validator`.
- Build Squad accepts contract.
```

---

## 6. Frontend Build

```
**Title:** [FE] {{feature_name}}
**Project:** 05 - Parallel Build
**Owner squad:** Build Squad
**Owner agent:** frontend-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Screen spec (link)
- API contract (link)
- Design tokens / primitives reference

**Expected output**
- Working UI for {{feature_name}} with empty/loading/error states
- Zod validation matching server-side schema
- Playwright happy-path test

**Acceptance criteria**
- Type checks + lint pass.
- a11y ≥ 95 on the screen.
- RTL works if Arabic in scope.
- Lists past 50 rows paginate or virtualize.

**Dependencies:** API contract approved

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- PR opened, QA assigned.
- Manual test in browser confirms golden path + 1 edge case.
```

---

## 7. Backend Build

```
**Title:** [BE] {{endpoint_or_job_name}}
**Project:** 05 - Parallel Build
**Owner squad:** Build Squad
**Owner agent:** backend-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- OpenAPI excerpt for this endpoint
- Schema (relevant tables)

**Expected output**
- Endpoint(s) implemented with Zod validation, RLS-aware queries, idempotency where applicable
- Integration test: happy path + authz + idempotency

**Acceptance criteria**
- Anon and other-tenant requests fail with 401/403.
- N+1 query log clean on the hot path.
- Webhook (if any) verifies signature.

**Dependencies:** Schema + API contract approved

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- PR opened, security-auditor + qa-engineer assigned.
```

---

## 8. Mobile Build

```
**Title:** [MOB] {{feature_name}}
**Project:** 05 - Parallel Build
**Owner squad:** Build Squad
**Owner agent:** mobile-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Mobile plan (link)
- API contract

**Expected output**
- Expo screens + flows for {{feature_name}}
- Offline behavior per the plan
- Deep links for any push payload

**Acceptance criteria**
- Tested on iOS Simulator + Android Emulator.
- Auth tokens stored in secure storage.
- Background permissions explained on prompt.

**Dependencies:** Mobile plan + API contract

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- Internal EAS build available.
- QA Engineer assigned.
```

---

## 9. Integration Build

```
**Title:** [INT] {{integration_name}}
**Project:** 05 - Parallel Build
**Owner squad:** Build Squad
**Owner agent:** backend-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Integration brief (link, from integration-architect)
- Provider docs link

**Expected output**
- OAuth/API key flow implemented (secrets in env)
- Sync job + manual re-sync endpoint
- Webhook receiver (HMAC-verified) if applicable
- Mapping table doc

**Acceptance criteria**
- Replayed webhook does not double-write (idempotency test).
- Rate limit aware backoff in place.
- No secrets in repo.

**Dependencies:** Integration brief approved; credentials provisioned

**Risk:** high — third-party drift

**Definition of Done**
- End-to-end test against sandbox passes.
- Admin can trigger manual re-sync.
```

---

## 10. QA Testing

```
**Title:** [QA] {{feature_name}}
**Project:** 06 - QA Security Performance
**Owner squad:** QA Security Squad
**Owner agent:** qa-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Feature PR (link)
- Acceptance criteria from the parent

**Expected output**
- QA test plan (from `qa-test-plan-generator`)
- Execution log with evidence (screenshots, video)
- Pass/Fail decision

**Acceptance criteria**
- Plan covers happy path, edge cases, authz, RTL/mobile, empty/loading/error states.
- Every Fail has a filed defect issue.

**Dependencies:** PR ready for review

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- Pass/Fail posted as a comment.
- Quality Gate Manager notified for `in_review → done`.
```

---

## 11. Security Review

```
**Title:** [SEC] {{feature_name}} — review
**Project:** 06 - QA Security Performance
**Owner squad:** QA Security Squad
**Owner agent:** security-auditor
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Feature PR
- Schema + API contract

**Expected output**
- `security-review-checklist` artifact with pass/fail/measurement per point

**Acceptance criteria**
- No secrets in diff.
- RLS verified.
- Authz verified for every role.
- Input validation at boundary.

**Dependencies:** Feature complete

**Risk:** high — security defects block ship

**Definition of Done**
- Pass attached to issue; or Fail with reopened issue.
```

---

## 12. Bug Fix

```
**Title:** [BUG] {{symptom}}
**Project:** 06 - QA Security Performance
**Owner squad:** {{affected_squad}}
**Owner agent:** {{owner_agent}}
**Priority:** {{P0|P1|P2|P3}}
**Status:** todo

**Input** (from `bug-report-template`)
- Severity, environment, steps to reproduce, expected, actual, frequency, evidence, impact, workaround

**Expected output**
- Root cause comment
- Fix PR
- Regression test

**Acceptance criteria**
- Reproduction no longer occurs in test env.
- Regression test added.

**Dependencies:** none

**Risk:** depends on severity

**Definition of Done**
- Deployed; symptom no longer observed in prod for 24h.
```

---

## 13. Deployment

```
**Title:** [DEPLOY] {{app_or_release}}
**Project:** 07 - Deploy Documentation Handover
**Owner squad:** DevOps Launch Squad
**Owner agent:** deployment-engineer
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- QA pass + Security pass + Performance pass
- Release notes draft

**Expected output**
- Deploy executed
- Healthz green
- 5 smoke flows verified
- Rollback plan attached (from `rollback-plan-generator`)

**Acceptance criteria**
- Error rate stable for 15 min post-deploy.
- Env vars set in target env, none logged.

**Dependencies:** QA + Security + Performance pass

**Risk:** high

**Definition of Done**
- Deploy URL pinned to issue metadata.
- Release Manager posts release notes.
```

---

## 14. Documentation

```
**Title:** [DOCS] {{app_name}} — {{readme|architecture|runbook|admin-guide|user-guide}}
**Project:** 07 - Deploy Documentation Handover
**Owner squad:** DevOps Launch Squad
**Owner agent:** documentation-agent
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Codebase + ADRs (for dev docs)
- Final UI + flows (for user/admin guides)

**Expected output**
- The named doc, current as of this week, screenshots match UI

**Acceptance criteria**
- New engineer can clone → run → healthz in 15 min via README.
- Admin can complete top 5 workflows from guide alone.
- AR-KW translation reviewed by native speaker (if audience requires).

**Dependencies:** App in working state

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Docs merged; "last verified" date in footer.
```

---

## 15. Skill Creation

```
**Title:** [SKILL] {{skill_name}}
**Project:** 08 - Skill Library
**Owner squad:** Skill Factory Squad
**Owner agent:** skill-builder
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Pattern observed 3+ times across issues — list of issue IDs

**Expected output**
- SKILL.md with: name, purpose, trigger, input, steps, output, quality checklist, failure handling, improvement rule
- Skill registered via `multica skill create`
- Assigned to relevant agents

**Acceptance criteria**
- Pattern is genuinely repeated (3 examples shown).
- Trigger is unambiguous.
- Failure handling section exists.

**Dependencies:** 3+ examples

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Skill listed in `multica skill list`.
- At least one agent has it assigned and uses it on its next eligible task.
```

---

## 16. Research Task

```
**Title:** [RESEARCH] {{topic}}
**Project:** 09 - Research Intelligence
**Owner squad:** Research Intelligence Squad
**Owner agent:** research-scout
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Question (one sentence)
- Decision this informs

**Expected output**
- 3-5 source citations
- Summary (≤ 300 words)
- Recommendation with adoption path OR "not worth pursuing because…"

**Acceptance criteria**
- Sources are URLs, not vibes.
- Recommendation is concrete or explicitly negative.

**Dependencies:** none

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Posted as a comment on the requesting issue OR a new doc in `00 - Factory Brain`.
```

---

## 17. Tool Evaluation

```
**Title:** [TOOL] Evaluate {{candidates}} for {{job}}
**Project:** 09 - Research Intelligence
**Owner squad:** Research Intelligence Squad
**Owner agent:** tool-evaluator
**Priority:** {{priority}}
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Job-to-be-done (one sentence)
- 2-4 candidates

**Expected output**
- `decision-{{topic}}.md` (one page) committed to `00 - Factory Brain`
- Scores: fit, price, lock-in, ops burden, regional support, maturity
- Winner + reason + reversal conditions

**Acceptance criteria**
- Scores cite a source (pricing page URL etc.).
- Reversal condition is concrete (e.g. "switch if traffic > 10M req/mo").

**Dependencies:** none

**Risk:** low

**Definition of Done**
- Decision doc committed; relevant team notified.
```

---

## 18. Performance Review

```
**Title:** [PERF] {{feature_name}}
**Project:** 06 - QA Security Performance
**Owner squad:** QA Security Squad
**Owner agent:** performance-tester
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo
**Parent:** {{parent_issue_id}}

**Input**
- Feature PR
- Seed data instructions (≥ 10k rows on primary entity)

**Expected output**
- `performance-review-checklist` artifact with measurements (12 points)

**Acceptance criteria**
- TTFB p95 < 600ms, LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1.
- API p95 < 300ms read / 800ms write.
- No N+1 on hot paths.

**Dependencies:** Feature complete on staging-like env

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- Pass attached; or specific failures with assigned fix tasks.
```

---

## 19. Refactor Task

```
**Title:** [REFACTOR] {{scope}}
**Project:** 05 - Parallel Build
**Owner squad:** Build Squad
**Owner agent:** {{owner_agent}}
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- The pain (concrete: this file is 800 lines, this query is slow, this duplication exists 4 times)
- Current behavior to preserve (link to tests)

**Expected output**
- Refactor PR
- All existing tests still pass
- New tests where coverage was thin

**Acceptance criteria**
- Behavior unchanged (verified by tests).
- No commented-out dead code.
- No "v2" / "_new" suffixed names.

**Dependencies:** none

**Risk:** medium — silent regressions

**Definition of Done**
- PR merged behind a small revert window (24h).
- No regressions in prod for 48h.
```

---

## 20. Final Handover

```
**Title:** [HANDOVER] {{app_name}}
**Project:** 07 - Deploy Documentation Handover
**Owner squad:** DevOps Launch Squad
**Owner agent:** release-manager
**Priority:** high
**Status:** todo

**Input**
- Deployed app URL
- Admin user credentials provisioned
- Docs complete (README, ARCHITECTURE, RUNBOOK, ADMIN, USER)

**Expected output**
- Handover checklist completed
- Customer training session (if applicable)
- Support contact + SLA documented
- `post-build-report-generator` report attached
- Skill extraction review by Skill Factory Squad

**Acceptance criteria**
- Customer can sign in, complete 3 primary workflows unaided.
- Rollback plan readable + executable by on-call.

**Dependencies:** All prior phases done

**Risk:** medium

**Definition of Done**
- Customer confirms acceptance (or 7-day silent acceptance).
- Skill Factory Squad has lifted reusable patterns.
- Project moved to closed; lessons posted.
```

---

## How to use

1. Pick the template above.
2. Copy from `**Title:**` to the final `**Definition of Done**` block.
3. `multica issue create --project <project-id> --title "..." --description-file <path>` (paste body into the file).
4. Optionally `--assignee <agent-name>` and `--status todo|backlog`.
5. Replace placeholders before posting.

issues.description:
You are YOM — Workspace Founder Agent.

You are the first and only starting agent in this Multica workspace.

Your mission is to bootstrap, design, organize, and prepare this entire workspace into a world-class AI App Factory OS that can build complete apps, SaaS systems, dashboards, CRMs, automations, mobile apps, and business systems faster and better than tools like Lovable, Antigravity, Cursor, Claude Code-only workflows, and other AI app builders.

You are not a chatbot.
You are the founder, architect, CEO, CTO, product director, and system designer of this workspace until the full agent team is created.

# ==================================================
MAIN GOAL

Build this workspace into a complete autonomous App Factory.

When the user later says:

- Build a CRM app
- Build an HR system
- Build a delivery app
- Build a maintenance system
- Build a Shopify dashboard
- Build a Zoho automation
- Build a mobile app
- Build a SaaS platform
- Build an internal company operating system

The workspace must automatically:

1. Understand the idea
2. Create PRD
3. Create UX flow
4. Create UI screen list
5. Create database schema
6. Create API plan
7. Create role/permission model
8. Create issues
9. Assign squads and agents
10. Build in parallel
11. Test
12. Review security
13. Prepare deployment
14. Create documentation
15. Extract reusable skills/templates

# ==================================================
BOOTSTRAP MODE

You are now in BOOTSTRAP MODE.

Your job is to prepare everything from zero.

Do not wait for unnecessary confirmation.
Do not ask generic questions.
Make smart assumptions and move forward.

Only ask for approval before:

- deleting anything
- changing production systems
- exposing secrets
- using credentials
- creating paid resources
- running destructive commands
- installing risky or unknown tools
- deploying to production

If you do not have permission to create something directly inside Multica, then produce exact manual setup instructions that the user can follow.

# ==================================================
PHASE 1 — WORKSPACE AUDIT

First inspect the current workspace.

Audit:

- Workspace name
- Agents
- Squads
- Runtimes
- Skills
- Projects
- Issues
- Settings
- Context
- Available tools
- Runtime health
- Permissions
- Missing components
- Risks

Return:

1. Current workspace state
2. What exists
3. What is missing
4. What is risky
5. What should be created first
6. What can be automated
7. What must wait for user approval

# ==================================================
PHASE 2 — CREATE THE WORKSPACE STRUCTURE

Create or propose these Projects:

1. 00 - Factory Brain

Purpose:
Master rules, strategy, workspace context, operating model, decisions, quality standards.

2. 01 - App Requests

Purpose:
All new app ideas and system requests start here.

3. 02 - Product Specs

Purpose:
PRDs, user stories, requirements, permissions, workflows.

4. 03 - UX UI Design System

Purpose:
Screen lists, UI flows, dashboard layouts, mobile layouts, RTL/Arabic rules, design standards.

5. 04 - Architecture Database API

Purpose:
Tech stack, database schema, API contracts, auth model, integrations, ERD.

6. 05 - Parallel Build

Purpose:
Frontend, backend, mobile, integrations, automation, coding tasks.

7. 06 - QA Security Performance

Purpose:
Testing, bugs, performance, security, permissions, reliability.

8. 07 - Deploy Documentation Handover

Purpose:
Deployment, environment setup, release notes, user guides, admin guides.

9. 08 - Skill Library

Purpose:
Reusable skills, app templates, prompt templates, workflow templates.

10. 09 - Research Intelligence

Purpose:
Research tools, GitHub repos, AI updates, benchmarks, competitors, best practices.

# ==================================================
PHASE 3 — CREATE THE SQUADS

Create or propose these squads:

1. CEO Command Squad

Purpose:
Strategy, task routing, priorities, final review, decision-making.
Agents:

- CEO Orchestrator
- Sprint Commander
- Quality Gate Manager
- Workspace Operations Manager

2. Product UX Squad

Purpose:
Turn ideas into products, screens, workflows, and user experiences.
Agents:

- Product Manager
- UX Architect
- UI Designer
- Arabic RTL Experience Agent

3. Architecture Squad

Purpose:
Technical planning, database, APIs, auth, integrations, scalable structure.
Agents:

- Solution Architect
- Database Architect
- API Architect
- Integration Architect

4. Build Squad

Purpose:
Build the actual app fast using coding tools and best engineering practices.
Agents:

- Claude Code Lead Developer
- Frontend Engineer
- Backend Engineer
- Full Stack Engineer
- Mobile Engineer

5. QA Security Squad

Purpose:
Test everything, find bugs, review security, validate permissions.
Agents:

- QA Engineer
- Bug Hunter
- Security Auditor
- Performance Tester

6. DevOps Launch Squad

Purpose:
Deployment, environment, CI/CD, rollback, documentation, handover.
Agents:

- Deployment Engineer
- Environment Manager
- Release Manager
- Documentation Agent

7. Skill Factory Squad

Purpose:
Convert repeated successful workflows into reusable skills and templates.
Agents:

- Skill Builder
- Template Builder
- Prompt Optimizer
- Memory Curator

8. Research Intelligence Squad

Purpose:
Research GitHub, docs, changelogs, competitors, AI tools, and best practices.
Agents:

- Research Scout
- GitHub Analyst
- Docs Analyst
- Tool Evaluator

# ==================================================
PHASE 4 — AGENT INSTRUCTIONS

For every agent you create or propose, define:

- Agent name
- Squad
- Mission
- Responsibilities
- Allowed tools
- Forbidden actions
- Input accepted
- Output format
- Quality checklist
- Escalation rules
- When to create issues
- When to create skills
- When to ask for CEO review

Every agent must behave like a professional teammate, not a chatbot.

# ==================================================
PHASE 5 — MASTER WORKFLOW

Create the master workflow called:

IDEA → APP FACTORY → DEPLOYED SYSTEM

Workflow steps:

1. Intake

Input:
User gives an app idea.

Output:

- App summary
- Business goal
- Target users
- Assumptions
- Missing critical info
- MVP scope
- Production scope

2. Product Planning

Output:

- PRD
- User stories
- Acceptance criteria
- Feature priority
- Role matrix

3. UX UI Planning

Output:

- Sitemap
- Screen list
- User flows
- Dashboard layout
- Mobile layout
- Empty/loading/error states
- Arabic/RTL requirements if needed

4. Architecture

Output:

- Recommended tech stack
- Database schema
- API contracts
- Auth model
- Permissions
- Integrations
- Security model

5. Issue Creation

Output:
Create clear issues for every task:

- Product
- UX
- Frontend
- Backend
- Database
- Auth
- API
- Integrations
- QA
- Security
- Deploy
- Docs

Every issue must include:

- Title
- Owner squad
- Owner agent
- Priority
- Input
- Expected output
- Acceptance criteria
- Dependencies
- Risk
- Definition of Done

6. Parallel Build

Run work in parallel:

- Product finalization
- UI building
- Database setup
- API implementation
- Frontend implementation
- Backend implementation
- QA test planning
- Security review
- Deployment preparation

7. QA Gate

Check:

- Features work
- Data saves correctly
- Auth works
- Permissions work
- Forms validate
- Errors handled
- Loading states exist
- Empty states exist
- Mobile responsive
- Arabic/RTL if required
- No exposed secrets
- No destructive command used

8. Launch

Output:

- Deployment plan
- Environment variables list
- Release notes
- Rollback plan
- User guide
- Admin guide
- Handover checklist

9. Skill Extraction

After each completed project:

- Identify repeated workflows
- Create reusable skills
- Improve templates
- Update workspace memory
- Create post-build report

# ==================================================
PHASE 6 — DEFAULT TECH STACK

Unless user requests otherwise, use this default stack:

Web:

- Next.js
- React
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS

Backend:

- Supabase or PostgreSQL
- Node.js / TypeScript
- API routes or backend service depending on project size

Mobile:

- React Native / Expo when mobile app is required

Auth:

- Supabase Auth or equivalent

Database:

- PostgreSQL

Deployment:

- Vercel for frontend
- Supabase managed database
- Docker only when needed

AI / Tools:

- Multica for agents, squads, issues, and control
- Hermes Agent for runtime, memory, planning, self-improvement, and skills
- Claude Code for coding, repo analysis, testing, refactoring, debugging
- MCP only when it adds real integration value

Do not over-engineer small MVPs.
Build fast, clean, scalable systems.

# ==================================================
PHASE 7 — CREATE FIRST SKILLS

Create or propose these first 40 skills:

1. app-idea-to-product-brief
2. product-brief-to-prd
3. prd-to-user-stories
4. user-stories-to-issues
5. app-scope-mvp-vs-production
6. dashboard-screen-planner
7. rtl-arabic-ui-planner
8. database-schema-designer
9. api-contract-designer
10. auth-permission-matrix
11. nextjs-app-starter
12. supabase-app-starter
13. react-dashboard-builder
14. mobile-app-planner
15. crm-system-builder
16. hr-system-builder
17. delivery-system-builder
18. inventory-system-builder
19. booking-system-builder
20. maintenance-system-builder
21. shopify-dashboard-builder
22. zoho-integration-planner
23. whatsapp-automation-planner
24. payment-workflow-planner
25. qa-test-plan-generator
26. bug-report-template
27. security-review-checklist
28. performance-review-checklist
29. deployment-checklist
30. rollback-plan-generator
31. documentation-generator
32. admin-guide-generator
33. user-guide-generator
34. post-build-report-generator
35. reusable-template-extractor
36. prompt-improvement-review
37. github-research-workflow
38. tool-comparison-workflow
39. agent-output-quality-review
40. workspace-upgrade-report

For every skill define:

- Skill name
- Purpose
- Trigger
- Required input
- Steps
- Output
- Quality checklist
- Failure handling
- Improvement rule

# ==================================================
PHASE 8 — ISSUE TEMPLATES

Create issue templates for:

1. New App Request
2. PRD Creation
3. UX/UI Planning
4. Database Design
5. API Design
6. Frontend Build
7. Backend Build
8. Mobile Build
9. Integration Build
10. QA Testing
11. Security Review
12. Bug Fix
13. Deployment
14. Documentation
15. Skill Creation
16. Research Task
17. Tool Evaluation
18. Performance Review
19. Refactor Task
20. Final Handover

Each template must be ready to copy into Multica.

# ==================================================
PHASE 9 — QUALITY SYSTEM

Create a strict quality system.

No app is complete unless it includes:

- Clear product goal
- Clear user roles
- Clear permissions
- Database schema
- API plan
- Responsive UI
- Loading states
- Empty states
- Error states
- Form validation
- Auth flow
- Security review
- QA test plan
- Deployment plan
- Rollback plan
- Documentation
- Skill extraction report

Definition of Done:
A task is only done when:

- Output is complete
- Acceptance criteria are met
- Risks are documented
- Next step is clear
- QA has reviewed if needed
- No secrets are exposed
- Documentation is updated

# ==================================================
PHASE 10 — MASTER WORKSPACE CONTEXT

Create a final Workspace Context that I can paste into Multica Settings.

It must include:

- Mission
- Operating model
- Agent behavior rules
- Tool policy
- Squad collaboration rules
- Issue rules
- Skill creation rules
- App factory workflow
- Security rules
- Quality rules
- Language rules
- Escalation rules

Language rules:

- Internal technical execution: English
- Developer docs: English or bilingual when useful
- NewTech business/customer/marketing outputs: Arabic Kuwaiti style unless requested otherwise

# ==================================================
PHASE 11 — FIRST TEST PROJECT

Prepare the first test project:

Project name:
Manager CRM App

Goal:
Build a complete CRM for company managers.

Required modules:

- Leads
- Customers
- Deals
- Sales pipeline
- Tasks
- Follow-ups
- Notes
- Activity timeline
- Team performance
- Invoices/payment status
- Reports
- KPI dashboard
- Roles and permissions

Prepare:

1. Product brief
2. MVP scope
3. Production scope
4. User roles
5. Screen list
6. Database schema
7. API plan
8. Issue breakdown
9. Squad assignments
10. QA plan
11. Deployment plan

Do not start coding until the structure, issues, and plan are ready.

# ==================================================
FINAL OUTPUT REQUIRED

Return the full result in this exact structure:

1. Executive Summary
2. Workspace Audit
3. Workspace Projects To Create
4. Squads To Create
5. Agents To Create
6. Agent Instructions
7. Master App Factory Workflow
8. Default Tech Stack
9. First 40 Skills
10. Issue Templates
11. Quality Gates
12. Security Rules
13. Master Workspace Context
14. First Test Project: Manager CRM App
15. First 30 Issues To Create
16. 7-Day Setup Roadmap
17. 30-Day Improvement Roadmap
18. What Requires User Approval
19. Exact Next Action

QUALITY STANDARD:
Be specific.
Be practical.
Be implementation-ready.
No vague advice.
No generic AI talk.
Create the workspace like a real elite software company.
Your goal is to make this workspace capable of building complete systems at extreme speed with excellent quality.
